907	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
908	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
909	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
910	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
911	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
912	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
913	//
914	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
915	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
916	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
917	// and stays bounded.
918	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
919		root := t.TempDir()
920		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
921		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
922		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
923	
924		// Seed commit so we have a base rev.
925		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
926			t.Fatalf("write seed: %v", err)
927		}
928		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "README.md")
929		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "seed")
930	
931		// Synthetic evidence commit that adds a multi-thousand-line session log
932		// PLUS a legitimate implementation change. The fix must exclude the
933		// session log from the diff while keeping the implementation change.
934		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "20260417T000000-testattempt", "embedded")
935		if err := os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
936			t.Fatalf("mkdir evidence: %v", err)
937		}
938		var bigLog strings.Builder
939		for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
940			fmt.Fprintf(&bigLog, "{\"seq\":%d,\"event\":\"tool_call\",\"payload\":\"lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit\"}\n", i)
941		}
942		sessionLogPath := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "agent-123.jsonl")
943		if err := os.WriteFile(sessionLogPath, []byte(bigLog.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
944			t.Fatalf("write session log: %v", err)
945		}
946	
947		// Legitimate implementation change that MUST survive in the diff.
948		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "implementation.go"), []byte("package main\n\nfunc Added() {}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
949			t.Fatalf("write implementation: %v", err)
950		}
951	
952		// Force-add the evidence (the pre-fix landEvidence behavior) plus the real change.
953		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "-f", ".ddx/executions/")
954		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "implementation.go")
955		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add execution evidence [testattempt] + impl")
956	
957		// Get the HEAD sha (the evidence commit).
958		headSha := strings.TrimSpace(runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
959	
960		// Call the gitShow method with the fix in place.
961		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{ProjectRoot: root}
962		out, err := reviewer.gitShow(headSha)
963		if err != nil {
964			t.Fatalf("gitShow: %v", err)
965		}
966	
967		// Must NOT include the session log content.
968		if strings.Contains(out, "lorem ipsum dolor sit amet") {
969			t.Errorf("gitShow output includes embedded session log content (pathspec exclusion not applied)")
970		}
971	
972		// Must include the legitimate implementation change.
973		if !strings.Contains(out, "implementation.go") {
974			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the legitimate implementation file (pathspec too aggressive)")
975		}
976		if !strings.Contains(out, "func Added()") {
977			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the implementation change body")
978		}
979	
980		// Must be bounded in size. The raw session log was ~1MB; fixed diff
981		// should be under 50KB easily (seed + impl.go + evidence metadata).
982		if len(out) > 150_000 {
983			t.Errorf("gitShow output size %d exceeds 150KB budget — pathspec exclusion did not bound the diff", len(out))
984		}
985	}

23	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
24	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
25	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
26	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration
33		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
34		EventSink       io.Writer
35		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
36		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
37		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
65		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
66		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
67		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
68		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
69		Mode         executeloop.Mode
70		IdleInterval time.Duration
71		Once         bool
72		PollInterval time.Duration
73		NoReview     bool
74		LabelFilter  string
75		SessionID    string
76		WorkerID     string
77		ProjectRoot  string
78		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
79		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
80		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
81		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
82		TargetBeadID string
83		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
84		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
85		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
86		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
87		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
88		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
89		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
90	
91		// OnAttemptFinalized, when non-nil, is called once per finalized attempt
92		// immediately after the report is appended to loop results. Best-effort:
93		// the hook must not block and its errors are silently discarded.
94		OnAttemptFinalized func(report ExecuteBeadReport)
95	}
96	
97	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
98		mode := r.Mode
99		if mode == "" {
100			if r.Once {
101				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
102			} else {
103				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
104			}
105		}
106		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
107		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
108			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
109		}
110		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
111			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
112		}
113		return mode, idleInterval
114	}
115	
116	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
117	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
118	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
119	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
120	
121	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
122	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
123	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
124	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
125	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
126	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
127	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
128	
129	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
130	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
131	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
132	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
133	// advance the base branch.
134	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
135	
136	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
137	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
138	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
139	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
140	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
141	// worker.
142	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
143	
144	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
145	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
146	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
147	// triage:needs-investigation park.
148	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
149	
150	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
151	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
152	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
153	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
154	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
155	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
156	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
157	//
158	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
159	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
160	//
161	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
162	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
163	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
164	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
165	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
166	// directly.
167	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
168		ctx context.Context,
169		projectRoot string,
170		b *bead.Bead,
171		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
172		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
173		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
174		actor, source string,
175		now func() time.Time,
176	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
177		if now == nil {
178			now = time.Now
179		}
180		if submit == nil {
181			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
182		}
183		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
184		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
185		if err != nil {
186			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
187			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
188			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
189			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
190			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
191				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
192			}
193			return &LandResult{
194				Status:      "preserved",
195				PreserveRef: ref,
196				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
197			}, nil, nil
198		}
199		if outcome.Blocked {
200			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
201			if perr != nil {
202				return nil, outcome, perr
203			}
204			if eventStore != nil {
205				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
206			}
207			return land, outcome, nil
208		}
209		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
210			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
211		}
212		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
213		return land, outcome, sErr
214	}
215	
216	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
217	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
218	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
219	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
220	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
221	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
222	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
223		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
224			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
225				return
226			}
227			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
228			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
229		}
230		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
231			if runtime.Log != nil {
232				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
233			}
234			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
235				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
236				"reason":  reason,
237			})
238			if berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, reason, at); berr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
239				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
240			}
241		}
242	
243		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
244		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
245		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
246		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
247			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
248				"depth": storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate),
249				"max":   maxDepth,
250			})
251			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
252				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
253				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
254				Body:      string(overflowBody),
255				Actor:     assignee,
256				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
257				CreatedAt: at,
258			})
259			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
260			return
261		}
262	
263		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
264		if err != nil {
265			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
266			return
267		}
268		if decomp == nil {
269			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
270			return
271		}
272		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
273		if lossyOrEmpty {
274			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
275			return
276		}
277	
278		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
279		if decompErr != nil {
280			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
281			return
282		}
283		if runtime.Log != nil {
284			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
285		}
286		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
287			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
288			"child_ids": childIDs,
289		})
290	}
291	
292	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
293	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
294	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
295	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
296	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
297		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
298		if hook == nil {
299			return report
300		}
301		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
302			return report
303		}
304		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
305		if err != nil {
306			if runtime.Log != nil {
307				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
308			}
309			return report
310		}
311		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
312		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
313		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
314		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
315			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
316		}
317		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
318			return report
319		}
320		if classificationRecognized {
321			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
322		}
323		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
324		return report
325	}
326	
327	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
328	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
329	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
330	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
331		if d <= 0 {
332			return 0
333		}
334		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
335			return MaxLoopCooldown
336		}
337		return d
338	}

349		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
350		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).

352		PredictedPower              int           `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`

354		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64       `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
355		PredictedCostSource         string        `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`

361		SessionID                   string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
362		BaseRev                     string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
363		ResultRev                   string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
364		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA before landing.
365		// Mirrors ExecuteBeadResult.ImplementationRev; empty for no-changes attempts.
366		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
367		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator landing.
368		// Distinct from ImplementationRev on the merge-commit path.
369		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
370		// EvidenceRev is the trailing evidence commit SHA when distinct from
371		// ImplementationRev. Empty when not separately committed.
372		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
373		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
374		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
375		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.

377		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
378		CycleIndex int    `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
379		RetryAfter string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
380		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
381		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
382		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
383		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
384		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
385		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
386		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
387		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
388		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
389		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
390		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
391		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
392		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
393		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
394		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
395		// compute wasted/effective spend.
396		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
397		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
398		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
399		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
400		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
401		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
402		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
403		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
404		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
405		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
406		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
407		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
408		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
409		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
410		// recommendation.
411		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
412		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
413		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
414		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
415		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
416		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
417		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
418		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
419		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
420		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
421		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
422		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
423		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
424		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
425		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
426		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
427		// occurring.
428		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
429		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
430		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
431		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
432		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
433		// ResourceExhausted carries the execution-root preflight result when the
434		// attempt stopped before the agent could safely continue.
435		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
436	}

561	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
562	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
563	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
564	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
565		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
566		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
567		SatisfactionChecker agenttry.SatisfactionChecker // nil → NoChangesContract default
568		// VerificationRunner runs a verification_command parsed out of a
569		// no_changes_rationale.txt under NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1). When
570		// nil, DefaultVerificationCommandRunner is used.
571		VerificationRunner agenttry.VerificationCommandRunner
572		Now                func() time.Time
573		// Reviewer is retained for legacy/manual review helper tests. Automated
574		// close eligibility is owned by pre-land candidate-cycle review, so the
575		// execute-loop success path no longer calls this post-land reviewer.
576		Reviewer BeadReviewer
577		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is consulted before a bead is claimed.
578		// The zero value fail-opens once and then allows.
579		ComplexityGate work.Guard
580	
581		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
582		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
583		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
584		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
585		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
586		// (escalating to land_conflict_operator_required); false means failed-but-retriable.
587		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
588	
589		// EscalationNextFloor, when non-nil, is called by the no-changes smart-retry
590		// path to advance the MinPower floor by exactly one ladder step above
591		// actualPower. Returns an error (e.g. ErrLadderExhausted) when already at the
592		// top tier, causing the bead to be parked to status=proposed.
593		EscalationNextFloor func(actualPower int) (int, error)
594	
595		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
596		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
597		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
598	}

600	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
601	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
602	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
603		if ch == nil {
604			return
605		}
606		select {
607		case ch <- evt:
608		default:
609		}
610	}

635	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
636		if w.Store == nil {
637			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
638		}
639		if w.Executor == nil {
640			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
641		}
642	
643		now := w.Now
644		if now == nil {
645			now = time.Now
646		}
647		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
648		if assignee == "" {
649			assignee = "ddx"
650		}
651		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
652		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
653			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
654		}
655		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
656		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
657			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
658		}
659		harness := rcfg.Harness()
660		provider := rcfg.Provider()
661		model := rcfg.Model()
662		profile := rcfg.Profile()
663		loopMode, idleInterval := runtime.loopIntent()
664	
665		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
666		resultsResetIdx := 0
667		complexityGuard := work.NewComplexityGuard(w.ComplexityGate, runtime.Log)
668		preclaimGuard := work.NewPreClaimGuard(runtime.PreClaimHook, w.Store, runtime.Log, now, 30*time.Second)
669		workLog := NewWorkLogRenderer(WorkLogRendererOptions{Now: now})
670		wasIdle := false
671		lastIdleQueueSignature := ""
672	
673		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
674			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
675		}
676	
677		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
678			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
679			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
680			"harness":      harness,
681			"model":        model,
682			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
683			"assignee":     assignee,
684			"mode":         string(loopMode),
685			"once":         loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce,
686		})
687		cleanupLog := runtime.CleanupLog
688		if cleanupLog == nil {
689			cleanupLog = runtime.Log
690		}
691		cleanupStop := startExecutionCleanupWorker(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, runtime.CleanupInterval, runtime.CleanupTickCh, cleanupLog, emit)
692		defer cleanupStop()
693		_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "startup")
694		// exitReason is populated as the loop exits to surface a structured reason
695		// in the loop.end event (ddx-dc157075 AC #4). Work-owned terminal reasons
696		// are classified through work.StopCondition; fatal_config,
697		// preflight_failed, resource_exhausted, and future provider exhaustion are
698		// still subsystem-specific exits.
699		exitReason := ""
700		setExit := func(condition, reason string) {
701			exitReason = reason
702			result.StopCondition = condition
703			result.ExitReason = reason
704		}
705		applyStop := func(input work.StopInput) bool {
706			decision, ok := work.ClassifyStop(input)
707			if !ok {
708				return false
709			}
710			setExit(string(decision.Condition), decision.ExitReason)
711			return true
712		}
713		if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
714			if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
715				detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
716					harness, model, rerr.Error())
717				if runtime.Log != nil {
718					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (startup)\n", detail)
719				}
720				emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
721					"harness": harness,
722					"model":   model,
723					"reason":  rerr.Error(),
724					"startup": true,
725				})
726				report := ExecuteBeadReport{
727					Status:           ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
728					Detail:           detail,
729					Harness:          harness,
730					Model:            model,
731					Disrupted:        true,
732					DisruptionReason: "preflight_rejected",
733					OutcomeReason:    "preflight_failed",
734				}
735				emitDisruptionDetected(emit, w.Store, "", "preflight_rejected", detail, harness, model, assignee, now().UTC())
736				result.Failures++
737				result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
738				result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
739				setExit("Preflight", "preflight_failed")
740				return result, nil
741			}
742		}
743		defer func() {
744			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
745				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
746				"successes":           result.Successes,
747				"failures":            result.Failures,
748				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
749				"exit_reason":         exitReason,
750			})
751		}()
752	
753		for {
754			_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "pre-claim")
755			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
756			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
757			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
758			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
759			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
760				if exitReason == "" {
761					applyStop(work.StopInput{ContextErr: err})
762				}
763				return result, err
764			}
765			if runtime.BudgetStop != nil {
766				if budgetReport, stopped := runtime.BudgetStop(); stopped {
767					applyStop(work.StopInput{Budget: true})
768					if budgetReport.Status == "" {
769						budgetReport.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
770					}
771					if budgetReport.Detail != "" && runtime.Log != nil {
772						_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, budgetReport.Detail)
773					}
774					result.Failures++
775					result.LastFailureStatus = budgetReport.Status
776					result.Results = append(result.Results, budgetReport)
777					return result, nil
778				}
779			}
780			if runtime.ResourceChecker != nil {
781				checkResult, checkErr := runtime.ResourceChecker.Check(ctx)
782				emitResourcePreflight(emit, "pre-claim", checkResult, checkErr)
783				logResourcePreflight(runtime.Log, "pre-claim", checkResult, checkErr)
784				if checkErr != nil {
785					var resourceErr *ResourceExhaustedError
786					if errors.As(checkErr, &resourceErr) {
787						resourceResult := resourceErr.Result
788						if resourceResult.ProjectRoot == "" && len(resourceResult.RootChecks) == 0 {
789							resourceResult = checkResult
790						}
791						report := ExecuteBeadReport{
792							WorkerID:          runtime.WorkerID,
793							Harness:           harness,
794							Model:             model,
795							Status:            ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted,
796							Detail:            ResourceExhaustedStopMessage,
797							Error:             resourceErr.Error(),
798							SessionID:         runtime.SessionID,
799							ResourceExhausted: &resourceResult,
800							Disrupted:         true,
801							DisruptionReason:  ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
802							OutcomeReason:     ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
803						}
804						result.Failures++
805						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
806						result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
807						setExit("ResourceExhausted", "resource_exhausted")
808						emitResourceExhausted(emit, nil, "", report, assignee, now().UTC())
809						if runtime.Log != nil {
810							_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, ResourceExhaustedStopMessage)
811						}
812						return result, nil
813					}
814					setExit("Preflight", "preflight_failed")
815					return result, checkErr
816				}
817			}
818

13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

**Label Handling:**
- Labels are read from `bead.Labels []string` 
- Used by `HasBeadLabel()` for exact match
- Used by `HasBeadLabelPrefix()` for prefix match
- `review:skip` + `review:skip-reason:*` pattern gates reviewer invocation

**Review Prompt Assembly:**
- Builds XML with bead, changed-files inventory, governing docs, ranked diff, optional prose
- Applies per-section evidence caps (FEAT-022)
- Pre-dispatch overflow check prevents oversize dispatch

**AC-Check Command:**
- Exists as `ddx bead ac-check` command (not built-in to loop)
- Mechanically classifies AC items and gathers evidence
- Output consumed by reviewer gate for structured input

23	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
24	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
25	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
26	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
27	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
28	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
29	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
30	
31	// ReviewerEscalatedEventKind is the kind:reviewer-escalated event kind
32	// appended when the reviewer's MinPower is bumped above the baseline
33	// impl.ActualPower+1 due to prior review-error or review-pairing-degraded
34	// events for the same result_rev.
35	const ReviewerEscalatedEventKind = "reviewer-escalated"
36	
37	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
38	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
39	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
40	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
41	type ImplementerRouting struct {
42		Harness     string
43		Provider    string
44		Model       string
45		ActualPower int
46		// Correlation is the implementer's correlation map. The reviewer copies
47		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
48		// aggregations can join the two calls.

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"

6		"os"

9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/accheck"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

1237	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
1238	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
1239	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
1240	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
1241	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
1242	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
1243	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
1244	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
1245		rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(r.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
1246		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, r.Service, r.Runner, rcfg, runtime)
1247	}

1249	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
1250	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
1251	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
1252	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
1253	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
1254		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1255		defer cancel()
1256		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1257		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1258		if err != nil {
1259			return "", err
1260		}
1261		return string(out), nil
1262	}

1265		if strings.TrimSpace(baseRev) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(resultRev) == "" {
1266			return "", fmt.Errorf("base_rev and result_rev are required")
1267		}
1268		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1269		defer cancel()
1270		args := append([]string{"diff", baseRev, resultRev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1271		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1272		if err != nil {
1273			return "", err
1274		}
1275		return string(out), nil
1276	}
1277	
1278	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
1279		if resultRev == "" {
1280			return ""
1281		}
1282		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
1283		if err != nil {
1284			return ""
1285		}
1286		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1287	}
1288	
1289	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
1290		if artifacts == nil {
1291			return nil
1292		}

1294			return err
1295		}
1296		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
1297	}
1298	
1299	func resultCost(result *Result) float64 {
1300		if result == nil {
1301			return 0
1302		}
1303		return result.CostUSD
1304	}

23	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
24	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
25	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
26	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration
33		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
34		EventSink       io.Writer
35		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
36		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
37		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
65		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
66		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
67		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
68		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
69		Mode         executeloop.Mode
70		IdleInterval time.Duration

72		PollInterval time.Duration

74		LabelFilter  string

79		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
80		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
81		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
82		TargetBeadID string
83		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
84		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
85		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
86		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
87		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
88		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
89		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
90	
91		// OnAttemptFinalized, when non-nil, is called once per finalized attempt
92		// immediately after the report is appended to loop results. Best-effort:
93		// the hook must not block and its errors are silently discarded.
94		OnAttemptFinalized func(report ExecuteBeadReport)
95	}

97	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
98		mode := r.Mode
99		if mode == "" {
100			if r.Once {
101				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
102			} else {
103				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
104			}
105		}
106		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
107		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
108			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
109		}
110		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
111			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
112		}
113		return mode, idleInterval
114	}
115	
116	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
117	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
118	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
119	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
120	
121	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
122	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
123	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
124	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
125	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
126	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
127	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
128	
129	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
130	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
131	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
132	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
133	// advance the base branch.
134	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
135	
136	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
137	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
138	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
139	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
140	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
141	// worker.
142	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
143	
144	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
145	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
146	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
147	// triage:needs-investigation park.
148	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
149	
150	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
151	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
152	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
153	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
154	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
155	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
156	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
157	//
158	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
159	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
160	//
161	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
162	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
163	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
164	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
165	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
166	// directly.
167	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
168		ctx context.Context,
169		projectRoot string,
170		b *bead.Bead,
171		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
172		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
173		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
174		actor, source string,
175		now func() time.Time,
176	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
177		if now == nil {
178			now = time.Now
179		}
180		if submit == nil {
181			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
182		}
183		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
184		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
185		if err != nil {
186			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
187			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
188			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
189			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
190			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
191				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
192			}
193			return &LandResult{
194				Status:      "preserved",
195				PreserveRef: ref,
196				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
197			}, nil, nil
198		}
199		if outcome.Blocked {
200			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})

358		Error                       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
359		Stderr                      string        `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
360		RateLimitBudget             time.Duration `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
361		SessionID                   string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
362		BaseRev                     string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
363		ResultRev                   string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
364		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA before landing.
365		// Mirrors ExecuteBeadResult.ImplementationRev; empty for no-changes attempts.
366		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
367		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator landing.
368		// Distinct from ImplementationRev on the merge-commit path.
369		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
370		// EvidenceRev is the trailing evidence commit SHA when distinct from
371		// ImplementationRev. Empty when not separately committed.
372		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
373		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
374		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.
375		// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.
376		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
377		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
378		CycleIndex int    `json:"cycle_index,omitempty"`
379		RetryAfter string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
380		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
381		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
382		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
383		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
384		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
385		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
386		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
387		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
388		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
389		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
390		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
391		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
392		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
393		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
394		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
395		// compute wasted/effective spend.
396		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
397		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
398		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
399		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
400		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
401		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
402		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
403		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
404		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
405		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
406		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
407		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
408		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
409		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
410		// recommendation.
411		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
412		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
413		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
414		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
415		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
416		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
417		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
418		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
419		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
420		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
421		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
422		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
423		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
424		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
425		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
426		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
427		// occurring.
428		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
429		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
430		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
431		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
432		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
433		// ResourceExhausted carries the execution-root preflight result when the
434		// attempt stopped before the agent could safely continue.
435		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
436	}
437	
438	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
439		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
440	}
441	
442	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
443	
444	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
445		return f(ctx, beadID)
446	}
447	
448	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
449		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
450		Get(id string) (*bead.Bead, error)
451		Create(b *bead.Bead) error
452		Claim(id, assignee string) error
453		Unclaim(id string) error
454		TouchClaimHeartbeat(id string) error
455		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
456		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
457		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
458		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail, baseRev string) error
459		AppendNotes(id string, notes string) error
460		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
461		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
462		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by review workflows that
463		// intentionally transition a bead back into open state.
464		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
465		// Update mutates a bead in place. Used by the post-merge triage step to
466		// add metadata hints (e.g. tier-pin) when the triage policy escalates after
467		// repeated review BLOCKs.
468		Update(id string, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
469		UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(id string, status string, opts bead.LifecycleTransitionOptions, mutate func(*bead.Bead) error) error
470		ParkToProposed(id string, reason bead.ParkReason, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
471	}
472	
473	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
474	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
475	type readyDiagnoser interface {
476		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
477	}
478	
479	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty.
480	// Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when NoReadyWork fires and the store
481	// exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
482	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
483		ExecutionReady            []string `json:"execution_ready,omitempty"`
484		DependencyWaiting         []string `json:"dependency_waiting,omitempty"`
485		ProposedOperatorAttention []string `json:"proposed_operator_attention,omitempty"`
486		RetryCooldown             []string `json:"retry_cooldown,omitempty"`
487		ExternalBlocked           []string `json:"external_blocked,omitempty"`
488		NotEligible               []string `json:"not_eligible,omitempty"`
489		Superseded                []string `json:"superseded,omitempty"`
490		Epics                     []string `json:"epics,omitempty"`
491		EpicClosureCandidates     []string `json:"epic_closure_candidates,omitempty"`
492		NextRetryAfter            string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
493	}
494	
495	// QueueSnapshot is the structured count-oriented companion to the legacy
496	// no_ready_work_detail ID lists.
497	type QueueSnapshot struct {
498		ExecutionReadyCount            int                          `json:"execution_ready_count"`
499		BlockedCount                   int                          `json:"blocked_count"`
500		DependencyWaitingCount         int                          `json:"dependency_waiting_count"`
501		ExternalBlockedCount           int                          `json:"external_blocked_count"`
502		ProposedOperatorAttentionCount int                          `json:"proposed_operator_attention_count"`
503		HumanReviewBlockerCount        int                          `json:"human_review_blocker_count"`
504		HumanReviewBlockedTotal        int                          `json:"human_review_blocked_total"`
505		HumanReviewBlockers            []HumanReviewBlockerSnapshot `json:"human_review_blockers,omitempty"`
506		RetryCooldownCount             int                          `json:"retry_cooldown_count"`
507		NextRetryAfter                 string                       `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
508		ExecutionIneligibleCount       int                          `json:"execution_ineligible_count"`
509		SupersededCount                int                          `json:"superseded_count"`
510		SkippedEpicsCount              int                          `json:"skipped_epics_count"`
511		EpicClosureCandidatesCount     int                          `json:"epic_closure_candidates_count"`
512	}
513	
514	// HumanReviewBlockerSnapshot reports one open human-review blocker and how
515	// many active downstream beads are transitively waiting on it.
516	type HumanReviewBlockerSnapshot struct {
517		ID                     string `json:"id"`
518		Title                  string `json:"title"`
519		Priority               int    `json:"priority"`
520		DownstreamBlockedCount int    `json:"downstream_blocked_count"`
521	}
522	
523	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
524	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
525	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
526	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
527	// these directly from the channel.
528	//
529	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
530	type ProgressEvent struct {
531		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
532		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
533		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
534		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`
535		BeadID    string    `json:"bead_id"`

1410		// no_changes signal when the agent also wrote the explicit rationale file.
1411		// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) will
1412		// overwrite Outcome and Status with the landing decision before output.
1413		agentReportedError := strings.TrimSpace(agentErrMsg) != ""
1414		switch {
1415		case exitCode != 0 || agentReportedError:
1416			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
1417		case resultRev == baseRev:
1418			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges
1419		default:
1420			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
1421		}
1422	
1423		// When the outcome is no_changes, attempt to read the agent's rationale file.
1424		// The agent is instructed to write this file (relative to the worktree) when it
1425		// determines the bead's work is already present. We read it before the deferred
1426		// worktree cleanup removes the file.
1427		if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges {
1428			rationaleFile := filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel, "no_changes_rationale.txt")
1429			if data, readErr := os.ReadFile(rationaleFile); readErr == nil {
1430				res.NoChangesRationale = strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
1431			}
1432			if res.NoChangesRationale == "" {
1433				res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence
1434				res.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
1435				paths := dirtyWorktreePaths(wtPath)
1436				res.NoEvidencePaths = paths
1437				if len(paths) > 0 {
1438					res.Error = fmt.Sprintf("%s; dirty paths: %s", res.Reason, strings.Join(paths, ", "))
1439				} else {
1440					res.Error = res.Reason
1441				}
1442			}
1443		}
1444	
1445		// Operator-cancel override (ADR-022 §Cancel SLA). When the mid-attempt
1446		// poll detected cancel-requested:true on the bead, force the result into
1447		// the preserved_for_review channel with reason "operator_cancel" so the
1448		// orchestrator and supervisor surface it as an operator-driven preserve
1449		// rather than a generic task_failed/no_evidence outcome.
1450		if cancelHonored != nil && cancelHonored.Load() {
1451			res.Outcome = "preserved"
1452			res.Reason = OperatorCancelReason
1453			res.Error = ""
1454			res.ExitCode = 0
1455			res.FailureMode = ""
1456			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
1457			res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, OperatorCancelReason, "")
1458			_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)
1459			return res, nil
1460		}
1461	
1462		// Classify failure mode from worker-level signals. ApplyLandingToResult
1463		// may refine this with landing-level signals (merge conflict, gate
1464		// failure) before the final result is output.
1465		res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
1466	
1467		// Record routing evidence on the bead (best-effort; errors are discarded).
1468		// Include requested passthrough constraints and power bounds so analytics
1469		// can separate intent from resolved outcome (AC5 of ddx-20047dd5).
1470		appendBeadRoutingEvidence(runtime.BeadEvents, beadID, resultHarness, resultProvider, resultModel, routeReason, routeBaseURL, rcfg.Passthrough(), rcfg.MinPower(), rcfg.MaxPower(), actualPower)
1471	
1472		// Record per-attempt cost evidence so cost rollup never has to join
1473		// against the session index. Best-effort; errors are discarded.
1474		appendBeadCostEvidence(runtime.BeadEvents, beadID, attemptID, costEventBody{
1475			Harness:      resultHarness,
1476			Provider:     resultProvider,
1477			Model:        resultModel,
1478			InputTokens:  inputTokens,
1479			OutputTokens: outputTokens,
1480			TotalTokens:  tokens,
1481			CostUSD:      costUSD,
1482			DurationMS:   res.DurationMS,
1483			ExitCode:     exitCode,
1484		})
1485	
1486		populateWorkerStatus(res)
1487		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res); err != nil {
1488			return nil, fmt.Errorf("writing execute-bead result artifact: %w", err)
1489		}
1490	
1491		// For the normal success path, commit the evidence bundle in the attempt
1492		// worktree so Land() can find it in the landing finalization worktree
1493		// without copying from the project root (AC: no live-worker noise in main).
1494		if resultRev != baseRev && exitCode == 0 {
1495			if newRev := commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel, attemptID); newRev != "" {
1496				resultRev = newRev
1497				res.ResultRev = newRev
1498				evidenceCommittedInWt = true
1499			}
1500		}
1501	
1502		// Optional out-of-band mirror of the bundle. Wired here so the whole

5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator

11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or

15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"

25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
29	const (
30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
40		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
41		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
42		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
43		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
44		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
45		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
46	)

48	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
49	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
50	// queue.
51	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
52	
53	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
54	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
55	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
56	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
57	//
58	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
59	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
60	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
61	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
62	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
63	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
64		switch outcome {
65		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
66			if exitCode == 0 {
67				return ""
68			}
69			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
70			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
71		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
72			return FailureModeNoChanges
73		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
74			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
75		}
76	
77		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
78		switch {
79		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
80			return FailureModeLockContention
81		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
82			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
83		case containsAny(lower,
84			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
85			"passthrough constraint:",
86			"max_power is less than min_power",
87			"max_power exceeds min_power",
88			"must be less than max_power",
89			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
90			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
91			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
92			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
93			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
94			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
95		case containsAny(lower,
96			"agent power unsatisfied",
97			"no model meets min_power",
98			"no model with power >=",
99			"minimum power not achievable",
100			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
101			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
102		case containsAny(lower,
103			"executable file not found in $path",
104			"executable file not found",
105			"no such file or directory") &&
106			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
107			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
108		case containsAny(lower,
109			"no viable harness",
110			"no harness configured",
111			"failed to initialize routing service",
112			"resolveroute:",
113			"no viable routing candidate",
114			"no live provider supports",
115			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
116			"no viable provider"):
117			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
118		case containsAny(lower,
119			"context length",
120			"context_length_exceeded",
121			"context window",
122			"maximum context",
123			"prompt is too long",
124			"prompt too long",
125			"token limit",
126			"too many tokens",
127			"exceeds the context"):
128			return FailureModeContextOverflow
129		case containsAny(lower,
130			"unauthorized",
131			"401 unauthorized",
132			"invalid api key",
133			"invalid_api_key",
134			"authentication failed",
135			"authentication_error",
136			"no api key",
137			"missing api key",
138			"quota exceeded",
139			"insufficient quota",
140			"insufficient_quota",
141			"rate limit",
142			"rate_limit",
143			"429"):
144			return FailureModeAuthError
145		case containsAny(lower,
146			"timeout",
147			"timed out",
148			"context deadline exceeded",
149			"deadline exceeded",
150			"signal: killed"):
151			return FailureModeTimeout
152		case containsAny(lower,
153			"merge conflict",
154			"ff-merge not possible",
155			"merge failed",
156			"automatic merge failed"):
157			return FailureModeMergeConflict
158		case containsAny(lower,
159			"build failed",
160			"compilation failed",
161			"compile error",
162			"compilation error",
163			"cannot find package",
164			"undefined:",
165			"undefined reference",
166			"syntax error"):
167			return FailureModeBuildFailure
168		case containsAny(lower,
169			"test failed",
170			"tests failed",
171			"--- fail:",
172			"assertion failed",
173			"test failure"):
174			return FailureModeTestFailure
175		}
176		if outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed && errMsg != "" {
177			return FailureModeUnknown
178		}

181		}
182		return ""

185	// IsWorktreeLostError reports whether errMsg describes the execute-bead
186	// infrastructure losing the isolated attempt worktree before DDx could inspect
187	// its HEAD. This deliberately requires both a HEAD/worktree signal and a
188	// missing-path signal so generic harness "no such file" errors do not get
189	// bucketed as infrastructure loss.
190	func IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg string) bool {
191		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
192		hasHeadSignal := containsAny(lower,
193			"failed to read worktree head",
194			"git rev-parse head",
195			"worktree path missing")
196		hasMissingPathSignal := containsAny(lower,
197			"chdir",
198			"no such file or directory",
199			"cannot access",
200			"does not exist")
201		return hasHeadSignal && hasMissingPathSignal
202	}
