204	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
205	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
206	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
207		for _, n := range needles {
208			if n == "" {
209				continue
210			}
211			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
212				return true
213			}
214		}
215		return false
216	}

218	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
219	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
220	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
221	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
222	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
223	// bead merged cleanly.
224	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
225		switch landingOutcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ""
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return FailureModeNoChanges
230		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
231			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
232		case "error":
233			if workerMode != "" {
234				return workerMode
235			}
236			return FailureModeUnknown
237		case "preserved":
238			switch {
239			case landingReason == "merge conflict" || landingReason == "merge failed" || landingReason == "ff-merge not possible":
240				return FailureModeMergeConflict
241			case landingReason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(landingReason, PreMergeChecksReason):
242				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
243			case landingReason == RatchetPreserveReason:
244				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
245			case landingReason == OperatorCancelReason:
246				return ""
247			}
248			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
249			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
250			if workerMode != "" {
251				return workerMode
252			}
253			return FailureModeUnknown
254		}
255		if workerMode != "" {
256			return workerMode
257		}
258		return FailureModeUnknown
259	}
260	
261	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
262	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
263	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
264	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
265	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
266	// test-oriented by convention.
267	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
268		for _, gr := range gateResults {
269			if gr.Status != "fail" {
270				continue
271			}
272			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
273			if containsAny(combined,
274				"build failed",
275				"compilation failed",
276				"compile error",
277				"compilation error",
278				"cannot find package",
279				"undefined:",
280				"undefined reference",
281				"syntax error") {
282				return FailureModeBuildFailure
283			}
284		}
285		return FailureModeTestFailure
286	}

288	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
289	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
290	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
291	const (
292		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
293		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
294		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
295		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
296	)
297	
298	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
299	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
300	const (
301		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
302		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
303		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
304		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
305		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
306		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
307		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
308		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
309		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
310		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
311		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
312		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
313		ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted          = "resource_exhausted"
314	
315		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
316		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
317		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
318		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
319		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
320		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
321		//
322		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
323		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
324		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
325		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
326		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
327		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
328	
329		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
330		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
331		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
332		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
333		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
334	
335		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
336		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
337		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
338		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
339		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
340	
341		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired is set when the focused
342		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
343		// operator judgment, not automated retry. The bead moves to status=proposed
344		// with a kind:land-conflict-operator-required event.
345		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired = "land_conflict_operator_required"
346	
347		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock is set when a BLOCK review verdict
348		// is classified as a terminal operator-required condition —
349		// review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth
350		// cap, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope. These classes do not re-enter
351		// the automated retry cycle and do not consume the no-progress retry
352		// budget; the bead moves to status=proposed until an operator
353		// intervenes.
354		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock = "review_terminal_block"
355	
356		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap is set when a BLOCK review verdict is
357		// classified as a fixable gap — the issues are addressable by a repair
358		// attempt at higher MinPower with the reviewer's rationale as context.
359		// Exactly one repair cycle is scheduled per result_rev; a second
360		// review_fixable_gap on the same result_rev falls through to the regular
361		// BLOCK triage path instead.
362		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap = "review_fixable_gap"
363	
364		// ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted is set when the candidate-cycle
365		// repair loop reaches its per-attempt repair_max_cycles limit before a
366		// reviewer approves the candidate. The latest candidate ref is retained for
367		// operator inspection.
368		ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted = "repair-cycle-exhausted"
369	)
370	
371	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
372	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
373	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
374	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
375		if exitCode != 0 {
376			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
377		}
378	
379		switch outcome {
380		case "merged":
381			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
382			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
383			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
384			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
385			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
386			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
387				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
388			}
389			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
390				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
391			}
392			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
393		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
394			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
395		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
396			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
397		case "error":
398			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
399		case "preserved":
400			switch {
401			case reason == "merge conflict" || reason == "merge failed" || reason == "ff-merge not possible":
402				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
403			case reason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(reason, PreMergeChecksReason):
404				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
405			case reason == RatchetPreserveReason:
406				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
407			case reason == OperatorCancelReason:
408				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
409			}
410			if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
411				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
412			}
413			// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
414			// explicitly requested no merge.
415			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
416		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
417			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
418		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
419			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
420		default:
421			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
422		}
423	}
424	
425	// IsResourceExhaustedStatus reports whether the status is the stable
426	// host-resource failure classification.
427	func IsResourceExhaustedStatus(status string) bool {
428		return status == ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted
429	}
430	
431	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
432		for _, prefix := range []string{
433			"evidence commit failed:",
434			"large-deletion gate:",
435			"syntax sanity gate:",
436			"post-land gate failed:",
437		} {
438			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
439				return true
440			}
441		}
442		return false
443	}
444	
445	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
446		switch {
447		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
448			return reason + ": " + errMsg
449		case reason != "":
450			return reason
451		case errMsg != "":
452			return errMsg
453		case status != "":
454			return status
455		default:
456			return "unknown"
457		}
458	}

data.text: Now I have enough context. Let me look at the execute_bead_review_terminal.go more broadly to understand how to add REQUEST_CLARIFICATION:

data.output:
1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
8	)
9	
10	// ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind is the event kind emitted when a BLOCK verdict
11	// is classified as terminal (requires human intervention, no automated retry).
12	const ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind = "review-terminal-block"
13	
14	// Terminal review class constants. A BLOCK review verdict whose rationale
15	// contains one of these labels is mapped to a terminal operator-required
16	// outcome instead of re-entering the automated retry cycle.
17	const (
18		ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap           = "review_spec_gap"
19		ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance = "review_missing_acceptance"
20		ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge          = "review_too_large"
21		ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope  = "review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope"
22	)
23	
24	// classifyTerminalReviewBlock returns the terminal class label if structured
25	// review evidence identifies a human-required condition, or "" if the BLOCK is
26	// a regular fixable gap that may enter the automated retry cycle.
27	func classifyTerminalReviewBlock(res *ReviewResult) string {
28		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictBlock {
29			return ""
30		}
31		classification := ClassifyReviewFindings(res)
32		switch classification.Class {
33		case ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap:
34			return ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap
35		case ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance:
36			return ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance
37		case ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge:
38			return ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge
39		case ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope:
40			return ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope
41		default:
42			return ""
43		}
44	}
45	
46	// reviewTerminalBlockEventBody builds the structured body for a
47	// ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind event, encoding the terminal class and
48	// result_rev so operators can triage without re-parsing reviewer text.
49	func reviewTerminalBlockEventBody(class, resultRev string) string {
50		return fmt.Sprintf("terminal_class=%s\nresult_rev=%s", class, resultRev)
51	}
52	
53	// applyTerminalReviewBlock records terminal block state on a bead: moves it to
54	// status=proposed with operator-required metadata and appends a
55	// review-terminal-block event. All store errors are best-effort — callers
56	// continue regardless so a store-side failure cannot strand the bead.
57	func applyTerminalReviewBlock(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, class, resultRev string) {
58		applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(
59			store,
60			beadID,
61			actor,
62			now,
63			"terminal review block: "+class,
64			"review terminal block requires operator decision",
65			"review terminal BLOCK and accept, split, block, or cancel",
66		)
67		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
68			Kind:      ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind,
69			Summary:   class,
70			Body:      reviewTerminalBlockEventBody(class, resultRev),
71			Actor:     actor,
72			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
73			CreatedAt: now.UTC(),
74		})
75	}
76	
77	func applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, reason, summary, suggestedAction string) {
78		_ = store.ParkToProposed(beadID, bead.ParkReviewTerminal, func(b *bead.Bead) {
79			// Migration-only cleanup: defensive removal for legacy rows that escaped
80			// the lifecycle migration or arrived via external import.
81			b.Labels = removeBeadLabels(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, bead.LabelNeedsInvestigation)
82			clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b)
83			bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
84				Reason:          reason,
85				Since:           now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
86				Source:          "ddx agent execute-loop",
87				SuggestedAction: suggestedAction,
88				Summary:         summary,
89			})
90		})
91	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	)
9	
10	// ExecuteBeadIterationBlock is the machine-readable JSON payload embedded in
11	// execute-bead iteration commit messages as the "ddx-iteration:" trailer value.
12	// All fields are projected from result.json; the struct is the canonical source
13	// for both commit-message rendering and forward-compatibility testing.
14	type ExecuteBeadIterationBlock struct {
15		BeadID              string  `json:"bead_id"`
16		AttemptID           string  `json:"attempt_id"`
17		SessionID           string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
18		Harness             string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
19		Provider            string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
20		Model               string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
21		TotalTokens         int     `json:"total_tokens"`
22		CostUSD             float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
23		BaseRev             string  `json:"base_rev"`
24		ResultRev           string  `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
25		RequiredExecSummary string  `json:"required_exec_summary"`
26		Outcome             string  `json:"outcome"`
27		ExecutionBundle     string  `json:"execution_bundle"`
28	}
29	
30	// BuildIterationCommitMessage renders the canonical execute-bead iteration
31	// commit message from res. All values are projected from the result — the same
32	// fields that are written to result.json — so the message is re-derivable from
33	// the tracked artifact file without re-running the agent.
34	//
35	// The message includes:
36	//   - A structured subject line referencing the bead ID
37	//   - The ddx-iteration JSON block (compact agent/provider metadata)
38	//   - The canonical Git trailer set (Ddx-Attempt-Id, Ddx-Worker-Id,
39	//     Ddx-Harness, Ddx-Model, Ddx-Result-Status)
40	//
41	// Unknown fields are handled deterministically: ResultRev is omitted when
42	// empty; RequiredExecSummary defaults to "skipped" when unset; WorkerID falls
43	// back to SessionID when no distinct worker identity exists.
44	func BuildIterationCommitMessage(res *ExecuteBeadResult) string {
45		block := ExecuteBeadIterationBlock{
46			BeadID:              res.BeadID,
47			AttemptID:           res.AttemptID,
48			SessionID:           res.SessionID,
49			Harness:             res.Harness,
50			Provider:            res.Provider,
51			Model:               res.Model,
52			TotalTokens:         res.Tokens,
53			CostUSD:             res.CostUSD,
54			BaseRev:             res.BaseRev,
55			ResultRev:           res.ResultRev,
56			RequiredExecSummary: iterationRequiredExecSummary(res),
57			Outcome:             res.Outcome,
58			ExecutionBundle:     res.ExecutionDir,
59		}
60	
61		blockJSON, err := json.MarshalIndent(block, "", "  ")
62		if err != nil {
63			// Fallback: minimal inline block so the commit is never trailer-free.
64			blockJSON = []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"bead_id":%q,"attempt_id":%q}`, res.BeadID, res.AttemptID))
65		}
66	
67		// Ddx-Worker-Id falls back to SessionID when no distinct worker identity exists.
68		workerID := res.WorkerID
69		if workerID == "" {
70			workerID = res.SessionID
71		}
72	
73		var b strings.Builder
74		b.WriteString("chore: execute-bead iteration ")
75		b.WriteString(res.BeadID)
76		b.WriteString("\n\nddx-iteration: ")
77		b.WriteString(string(blockJSON))
78		// Blank line between the JSON block and the canonical trailer set is required
79		// by git-interpret-trailers so that the trailers are parsed correctly.
80		b.WriteString("\n\nDdx-Attempt-Id: ")
81		b.WriteString(res.AttemptID)
82		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Worker-Id: ")
83		b.WriteString(workerID)
84		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Harness: ")
85		b.WriteString(res.Harness)
86		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Model: ")
87		b.WriteString(res.Model)
88		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Result-Status: ")
89		b.WriteString(res.Status)
90		return b.String()
91	}
92	
93	// iterationRequiredExecSummary returns the required_exec_summary value for the
94	// ddx-iteration block. When the orchestrator has not yet evaluated gates (i.e.
95	// the preliminary result is written before the synthesized commit), the field
96	// defaults to "skipped" per the documented contract. After ApplyLandingToResult
97	// the field is populated with the real summary and the final result.json is
98	// written.
99	func iterationRequiredExecSummary(res *ExecuteBeadResult) string {
100		if res.RequiredExecSummary != "" {
101			return res.RequiredExecSummary
102		}
103		return "skipped"
104	}
105	
106	// BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile reads the result.json artifact at resultPath
107	// and renders the canonical execute-bead iteration commit message from it.
108	// This is the file-sourced rendering path: callers must write result.json before
109	// calling this function. The message is deterministic for a given result.json —
110	// anyone can re-derive it from the tracked bundle without re-running the agent.
111	func BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(resultPath string) (string, error) {
112		raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
113		if err != nil {
114			return "", fmt.Errorf("reading result file %s: %w", resultPath, err)
115		}
116		var res ExecuteBeadResult
117		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
118			return "", fmt.Errorf("parsing result file %s: %w", resultPath, err)
119		}
120		return BuildIterationCommitMessage(&res), nil
121	}

16	)

18	type parkReasonMeta struct {
19		Reason string
20		Source string
21	}
22	
23	var parkReasonMetaMap = map[ParkReason]parkReasonMeta{
24		ParkIntakeRejection:           {Reason: "pre-claim intake blocked execution", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
25		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired: {Reason: "operator decision required before another automated attempt", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
26		ParkPostReviewMalfunction:     {Reason: "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
27		ParkReviewTerminal:            {Reason: "terminal review block requires operator decision", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
28		ParkConflictRecovery:          {Reason: "land conflict requires operator judgment", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
29	}
30	
31	// ParkToProposed transitions the bead to proposed status for operator
32	// attention. It enforces OperatorRequired=true and the canonical Reason and
33	// Source from the ParkReason mapping. The mutate callback runs after the
34	// status transition; pass nil if no additional metadata is needed.
35	func (s *Store) ParkToProposed(id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
36		meta, ok := parkReasonMetaMap[reason]
37		if !ok {
38			return fmt.Errorf("bead: unknown ParkReason %q", reason)
39		}
40		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, StatusProposed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
41			OperatorRequired: true,
42			Reason:           meta.Reason,
43			Source:           meta.Source,
44		}, func(b *Bead) error {
45			if mutate != nil {
46				mutate(b)
47			}
48			return nil
49		})
50	}

input.new_string:
// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.

## Strictness mode: <strictness-mode>

- strict (kind:fix, kind:feat): every AC requires named test evidence or explicit diff citation; build-gate and test-name ACs must name a Test* function.
- behavior-light (kind:refactor, kind:chore): pass = build green + symbol or file evidence; test-name match only required when the AC explicitly names a Test*.
- file-evidence-only (kind:doc, kind:mechanical): file/rename/symbol presence suffices; no test execution required.

## AC-check ratification

When an <ac-check> section is present, ratify each item's mechanical result:
- For pass/fail items: confirm the mechanical result OR provide an explicit judgment_override with diff citation.
- For needs_judgment items: adjudicate pass/fail based on the diff, OR emit REQUEST_CLARIFICATION if you cannot adjudicate without more bead context.

## Verdict

Assign one overall verdict:

- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented; or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
- REQUEST_CLARIFICATION — you cannot adjudicate one or more needs_judgment AC items without more bead context; routes to operator lane, does NOT block the drain queue.

## Required output format (schema_version: 1)

Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:

` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "verdict": "APPROVE",
  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
  "per_ac": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "item": "acceptance criterion text",
      "grade": "pass",
      "evidence": "file:line or test evidence",
      "judgment_override": { "reason": "why mechanical result was overridden", "diff_citation": "path/to/file.go:42" }
    }
  ],
  "findings": [
    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
  ]
}
` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `

Rules:
- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK", "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION".
- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
- judgment_override is only required when overriding a mechanical pass to fail; it must include a diff_citation.
- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, or REQUEST_CLARIFICATION anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`

275	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
276	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
277	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
278	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
279		Caps evidence.Caps
280	}
281	
282	// ReviewPromptOptions controls optional prompt sections.
283	type ReviewPromptOptions struct {
284		IncludeProse bool
285	}
286	
287	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
288	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
289	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
290	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
291	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
292		Prompt   string
293		Overflow bool
294		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
295	}
296	
297	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
298	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
299	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
300	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
301		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
302	}
303	
304	// BuildReviewPromptWithProse builds the review prompt and includes an
305	// advisory prose-quality section derived from the bead text using the same
306	// docprose checker and config path as `ddx doc prose`.
307	func BuildReviewPromptWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
308		return BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
309	}
310	
311	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
312	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
313	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
314	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
315	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
316	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
317	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
318	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
319		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, false)
320	}
321	
322	// BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse assembles the review prompt with the
323	// optional prose-quality section enabled.
324	func BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
325		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, true)
326	}
327	
328	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
329		caps := opts.Caps
330		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
331			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
332		}
333	
334		var sb strings.Builder
335		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
336	
337		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
338	
339		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
340		beadStart := sb.Len()
341		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
342		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
343	
344		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
345			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
346		} else {
347			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
348		}
349	
350		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
351			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
352		} else {
353			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
354		}
355	
356		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
357			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
358		}
359	
360		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
361			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
362		}
363	
364		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
365		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
366			Name:          "bead",
367			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
368			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
369		})
370	
371		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
372		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
373		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
374			invStart := sb.Len()
375			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
376			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
377			for _, f := range allFiles {
378				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
379				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
380			}
381			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
382			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
383				Name:          "changed-files",
384				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
385				SelectedItems: paths,
386			})
387		}
388	
389		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
390		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
391		if len(refs) == 0 {
392			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
393		} else {
394			for _, ref := range refs {
395				if ref.Title != "" {
396					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
397				} else {
398					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
399				}
400				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
401				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
402				if readErr != nil {
403					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
404					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
405						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
406						TruncationReason: "read_error",
407						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
408					})
409				} else {
410					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
411					if truncated {
412						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
413					}
414					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
415					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
416						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
417						BytesIncluded: len(content),
418						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
419					}
420					if truncated {
421						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
422						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
423					}
424					sections = append(sections, sec)
425				}
426				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
427			}
428		}
429		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
430	
431		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
432		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
433		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
434		diffSection.Name = "diff"
435		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
436		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
437	
438		if includeProse {
439			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
440			if proseErr != nil {
441				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
442				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
443				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
444				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
445					Name:          "prose-review",
446					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
447				})
448			} else {
449				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
450				sb.WriteString("\n")
451				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
452			}
453		}
454	
455		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
456		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
457		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
458		if includeProse {
459			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
460		}
461		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
462	
463		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
464	
465		out := sb.String()
466		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
467			Name:    "review-prompt",
468			Content: out,
469		}}, len(out))
470		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
471			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
472			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
473			Sections: sections,
474		}
475	}
476	
477	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
478		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
479		if err != nil {
480			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
481		}
482	
483		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
484		if err != nil {
485			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
486		}
487		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
488		if err != nil {
489			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
490		}
491	
492		var src strings.Builder
493		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
494			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
495		}
496		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
497			src.WriteString(desc)
498			src.WriteString("\n\n")
499		}
500		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
501			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
502			src.WriteString(acc)
503			src.WriteString("\n\n")
504		}
505		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
506			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
507			src.WriteString(notes)
508			src.WriteString("\n")
509		}
510	
511		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
512		var sb strings.Builder
513		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
514		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
515		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
516		if len(findings) == 0 {
517			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
518		} else {
519			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
520			for _, finding := range findings {
521				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
522				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
523				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
524				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
525			}
526			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
527		}
528		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
529	
530		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
531			Name:          "prose-review",
532			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
533		}
534		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
535		return sb.String(), section, nil
536	}
537	
538	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
539	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
540	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
541	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
542	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
543	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
544	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
545		if len(files) == 0 {
546			return originalDiff
547		}
548		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
549			if f.Path == "" {
550				return 3
551			}
552			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
553				return 0
554			}
555			for _, r := range refs {
556				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
557					return 1
558				}
559			}
560			return 2
561		}
562		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
563		copy(ordered, files)
564		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
565	
566		var b2 strings.Builder
567		for _, f := range ordered {
568			body := f.Body
569			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
570				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
571			}
572			b2.WriteString(body)
573		}
574		return b2.String()
575	}
576	
577	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
578	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
579	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
580	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
581	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
582		var b strings.Builder
583		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
584		b.WriteString(f.Path)
585		b.WriteString(" b/")
586		b.WriteString(f.Path)
587		b.WriteString("\n")
588		if f.Stat != "" {
589			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
590			b.WriteString("\n")
591		}
592		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
593			b.WriteString(h)
594			b.WriteString("\n")
595		}
596		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
597		return b.String()
598	}
599	
600	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
601	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
602	// exact symbolic resolution).
603	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
604		if text == "" || p == "" {
605			return false
606		}
607		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
608			return true
609		}
610		base := path.Base(p)
611		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
612	}
613	
614	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
615	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
616	// documents.
617	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
618		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
619			return false
620		}
621		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
622		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
623			return true
624		}
625		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
626			return true
627		}
628		return false
629	}
630	
631	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
632	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
633		var buf bytes.Buffer
634		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
635			"&", "&",
636			"<", "<",
637			">", ">",
638		).Replace(s))
639		return buf.String()
640	}

642	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
643	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
644	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
645	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
646		ProjectRoot string
647		BeadStore   BeadReader
648		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
649		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
650		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
651		Service agentlib.FizeauService
652		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
653		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
654		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
655		Runner AgentRunner
656		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
657		// When Harness is empty, the reviewer follows the implementation harness;
658		// when both are empty, dispatch is left to the agent service.
659		Harness string
660		Model   string
661		// ModelRef is retained for tests/legacy callers that explicitly want an
662		// upstream model-ref override. Default reviewer routing uses fizeau profiles.
663		ModelRef string
664		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
665		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
666		// applies.
667		Caps evidence.Caps
668		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
669		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
670		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
671		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
672		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
673		// EventReader, when non-nil, is used to count prior review escalation
674		// triggers (review-error and review-pairing-degraded events) for the
675		// same result_rev so the reviewer MinPower is bumped on retry.
676		EventReader BeadEventReader
677	}
678	
679	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
680	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
681	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
682	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
683	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
684	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
685	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
686	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
687		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
688	}
689	
690	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
691	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
692	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
693	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
694	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
695	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
696		correlation := map[string]string{}
697		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
698			correlation[k] = v
699		}
700		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
701		if meta.GroupID != "" {
702			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
703			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
704		}
705		if impl.Harness != "" {
706			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
707		}
708		if impl.Provider != "" {
709			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
710		}
711		if impl.Model != "" {
712			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
713		}
714		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
715			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
716		}
717		minPower := 0
718		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
719			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
720		}
721		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
722		return AgentRunRuntime{
723			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
724			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
725			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
726			Correlation:         correlation,
727			Role:                "reviewer",
728			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
729			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
730			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
731			ClearProfile:        true,
732			ClearMaxPower:       true,
733		}
734	}
735	
736	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerProfileForDispatch(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting) string {
737		return r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, 0).Name
738	}
739	
740	// reviewerDispatchProfile returns the fizeau profile name and MinPower to use
741	// for the reviewer dispatch. priorErrors is the number of prior review-error
742	// and review-pairing-degraded events for the same result_rev; each adds one
743	// ladder step to MinPower:
744	//   - 0 prior errors: impl.ActualPower+1 (baseline R4 pairing)
745	//   - 1 prior error:  impl.ActualPower+2 (one step above baseline)
746	//   - 2+ prior errors: top-of-ladder (SelectStrongestProfile, no floor)
747	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting, priorErrors int) reviewerDispatchProfile {
748		floor := 0
749		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
750			floor = impl.ActualPower + 1
751		}
752		// Escalated floor: each prior error adds one ladder step.
753		escalatedFloor := floor
754		if priorErrors > 0 && floor > 0 {
755			escalatedFloor = floor + priorErrors
756		}
757		if r.Runner != nil {
758			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
759		}
760		selectedSvc := r.Service
761		if selectedSvc == nil {
762			factory := serviceRunFactory
763			if factory == nil {
764				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
765			}
766			built, err := factory(r.ProjectRoot)
767			if err != nil {
768				return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
769			}
770			selectedSvc = built
771		}
772		snap, err := LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx, selectedSvc)
773		if err != nil {
774			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
775		}
776		// On 2+ prior errors (or 1+ error with unknown impl power), use the
777		// strongest available profile without a floor restriction (top-of-ladder).
778		topOfLadder := priorErrors >= 2 || (priorErrors >= 1 && floor == 0)
779		name := ""
780		if topOfLadder {
781			name = SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
782		} else if escalatedFloor > 0 {
783			name = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, escalatedFloor)
784		} else {
785			name = selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap)
786		}
787		if name == "" {
788			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: escalatedFloor}
789		}
790		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
791			if profile.Name == name {
792				// Use the higher of the profile's own MinPower and the escalated floor
793				// so top-of-ladder selection never yields a MinPower below the floor
794				// we computed from the error count.
795				mp := profile.MinPower
796				if escalatedFloor > mp {
797					mp = escalatedFloor
798				}
799				return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: mp}
800			}
801		}
802		return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: escalatedFloor}
803	}
804	
805	func selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string {
806		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
807			if profile.Name == "smart" && profileHasAvailableModel(profile, snap.Models) {
808				return profile.Name
809			}
810		}
811		return SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
812	}
813	
814	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) applyExplicitReviewerPins(runtime *AgentRunRuntime) string {
815		if runtime == nil {
816			return ""
817		}
818		if r.Model != "" {
819			runtime.ModelOverride = r.Model
820			return r.Model
821		}
822		if r.ModelRef != "" {
823			runtime.ModelRefOverride = r.ModelRef
824			return r.ModelRef
825		}
826		return runtime.ProfileOverride
827	}
828	
829	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
830		if len(correlation) == 0 {
831			return ""
832		}
833		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
834		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
835			parts = append(parts, beadID)
836		}
837		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
838			parts = append(parts, groupID)
839		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
840			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
841		}
842		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
843			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
844		}
845		if len(parts) == 0 {
846			return ""
847		}
848		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
849	}
850	
851	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
852	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
853	// operators can see exactly which routes converged. resultRev is included so
854	// the event can be scoped to a specific commit when counting escalation
855	// triggers.
856	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int, resultRev string) string {
857		var b strings.Builder
858		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
859		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
860		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
861		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
862		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
863		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
864		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
865		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
866		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
867		if resultRev != "" {
868			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
869		}
870		return b.String()
871	}
872	
873	// countPriorEscalationTriggers counts review-error and review-pairing-degraded
874	// events recorded against the bead whose body cites the given result_rev. Each
875	// counts as one escalation step — review-error events from transport/overflow/
876	// parse failures and pairing-degraded events from routing collisions both
877	// signal that the current reviewer tier is insufficient.
878	func countPriorEscalationTriggers(reader BeadEventReader, beadID, resultRev string) int {
879		if reader == nil || resultRev == "" {
880			return 0
881		}
882		events, err := reader.Events(beadID)
883		if err != nil {
884			return 0
885		}
886		n := 0
887		for _, ev := range events {
888			if ev.Kind != "review-error" && ev.Kind != ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind {
889				continue
890			}
891			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
892			if m == nil || m[1] != resultRev {
893				continue
894			}
895			n++
896		}
897		return n
898	}
899	
900	// reviewerEscalatedEventBody renders the kind:reviewer-escalated event body.
901	func reviewerEscalatedEventBody(newMinPower, errorCount int, resultRev string) string {
902		var b strings.Builder
903		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "new_min_power=%d\n", newMinPower)
904		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "error_count=%d\n", errorCount)
905		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
906		return b.String()
907	}

909	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
910	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
911		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
912		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
913		if err != nil {
914			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
915		}
916		return r.reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl, diff, r.ProjectRoot)
917	}
918	
919	// Review implements CandidateReviewer for the pre-land candidate-cycle path.
920	// It reviews the full base_rev..candidate_rev range and dispatches the reviewer
921	// inside the still-live attempt worktree.
922	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) Review(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateReviewResult, error) {
923		reviewer := *r
924		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
925			reviewer.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
926		}
927		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
928			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: project_root required")
929		}
930		beadID := candidate.Report.BeadID
931		if beadID == "" {
932			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: bead_id required")
933		}
934		diff, err := reviewer.gitDiff(candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev)
935		if err != nil {
936			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: git diff %s..%s: %w", candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev, err)
937		}
938		workDir := candidate.WorktreePath
939		if workDir == "" {
940			workDir = reviewer.ProjectRoot
941		}
942		impl := ImplementerRouting{
943			Harness:     candidate.Report.Harness,
944			Provider:    candidate.Report.Provider,
945			Model:       candidate.Report.Model,
946			ActualPower: candidate.Report.ActualPower,
947			Correlation: map[string]string{
948				"bead_id":       beadID,
949				"attempt_id":    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
950				"session_id":    candidate.Report.SessionID,
951				"result_rev":    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
952				"candidate_ref": candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
953				"cycle_index":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", candidate.CycleIndex),
954			},
955		}
956		group, groupErr := reviewer.reviewGroupWithDiff(ctx, beadID, candidate.Report.ResultRev, impl, diff, workDir)
957		review, reduceErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group)
958		if review == nil {
959			if reduceErr != nil {
960				return CandidateReviewResult{}, reduceErr
961			}
962			return CandidateReviewResult{}, groupErr
963		}
964		out := CandidateReviewResult{
965			Verdict:        string(review.Verdict),
966			Rationale:      review.Rationale,
967			PerAC:          append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
968			Findings:       append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
969			Classification: ClassifyReviewFindings(review).Class,
970		}
971		if reduceErr != nil {
972			return out, reduceErr
973		}
974		return out, groupErr
975	}
976	
977	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting, diff, reviewWorkDir string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
978		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
979		if err != nil {
980			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
981		}
982	
983		// Resolve governing document references.
984		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
985	
986		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
987		iter := 1
988	
989		// Count prior escalation triggers so reviewerDispatchProfile can bump
990		// MinPower on retries.
991		priorErrors := countPriorEscalationTriggers(r.EventReader, beadID, resultRev)
992	
993		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
994		caps := r.Caps
995		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
996			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
997		}
998		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
999		prompt := built.Prompt
1000		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
1001		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
1002		if err != nil {
1003			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
1004		}
1005		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
1006			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
1007		}
1008	
1009		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
1010		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
1011		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
1012		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
1013		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
1014		if built.Overflow {
1015			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1016			reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, priorErrors)
1017			reviewRouteLabel := reviewProfile.Name
1018			if r.Model != "" {
1019				reviewRouteLabel = r.Model
1020			} else if r.ModelRef != "" {
1021				reviewRouteLabel = r.ModelRef
1022			}
1023			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1024				Sections:    built.Sections,
1025				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1026				OutputBytes: 0,
1027				Harness:     r.Harness,
1028				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
1029			}
1030			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1031				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
1032				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1033				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1034				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
1035				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
1036				BaseRev:         baseRev,
1037				ResultRev:       resultRev,
1038				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
1039				CostUSD:         0,
1040				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
1041				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
1042			}
1043			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1044				Harness:          r.Harness,
1045				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
1046				BaseRev:          baseRev,
1047				ResultRev:        resultRev,
1048				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
1049				BeadID:           beadID,
1050				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1051				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
1052			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1053				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
1054				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1055				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
1056			})
1057			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
1058				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
1059		}
1060	
1061		// Resolve reviewer routing. An explicit reviewer harness/model wins as an
1062		// operator override; otherwise DDx sends only a fizeau profile hint plus
1063		// the reviewer MinPower floor.
1064		reviewHarness := r.Harness
1065		reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl, priorErrors)
1066		// Emit reviewer-escalated event when MinPower is bumped above baseline.
1067		if priorErrors > 0 && r.BeadEvents != nil {
1068			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1069				Kind:      ReviewerEscalatedEventKind,
1070				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer escalated to min_power=%d after %d prior error(s)", reviewProfile.MinPower, priorErrors),
1071				Body:      reviewerEscalatedEventBody(reviewProfile.MinPower, priorErrors, resultRev),
1072				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1073				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1074			})
1075		}
1076	
1077		start := time.Now()
1078		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewProfile.Name)
1079		// Apply escalated MinPower: use the higher of the base R4 floor and the
1080		// escalated profile floor so retries reach a stronger reviewer tier.
1081		if reviewProfile.MinPower > runRuntime.MinPowerOverride {
1082			runRuntime.MinPowerOverride = reviewProfile.MinPower
1083		}
1084		reviewRouteLabel := r.applyExplicitReviewerPins(&runRuntime)
1085		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
1086		runRuntime.WorkDir = reviewWorkDir
1087		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
1088	
1089		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
1090		if runErr != nil {
1091			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
1092			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
1093			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
1094			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1095				Sections:    built.Sections,
1096				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1097				OutputBytes: 0,
1098				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1099				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1100				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
1101			}
1102			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1103				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
1104				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
1105				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
1106				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
1107				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
1108				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
1109				ResultRev:       resultRev,
1110				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
1111				DurationMS:      durationMS,
1112				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
1113				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
1114				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
1115			}
1116			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1117				Harness:          reviewHarness,
1118				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
1119				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
1120				ResultRev:        resultRev,
1121				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1122				BeadID:           beadID,
1123				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1124				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1125			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1126				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1127				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
1128				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1129				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1130			})
1131			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
1132		}
1133	
1134		actualHarness := reviewHarness
1135		actualModel := reviewRouteLabel
1136		actualProvider := ""
1137		actualPower := 0
1138		if result != nil {
1139			if result.Harness != "" {
1140				actualHarness = result.Harness
1141			}
1142			if result.Model != "" {
1143				actualModel = result.Model
1144			}
1145			actualProvider = result.Provider
1146			actualPower = result.ActualPower
1147			durationMS = result.DurationMS
1148		}
1149	
1150		output := ""
1151		sessionID := ""
1152		if result != nil {
1153			output = result.Output
1154			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
1155		}
1156		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
1157		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
1158		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
1159		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop leaves the bead open for
1160		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
1161		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
1162		var strictVerdict Verdict
1163		var rationale string
1164		var findings []Finding
1165		if parseErr == nil {
1166			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
1167			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
1168			findings = parsed.Findings
1169			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
1170				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
1171				for _, f := range findings {
1172					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
1173					if line == "" {
1174						continue
1175					}
1176					if f.Location != "" {
1177						line = f.Location + ": " + line
1178					}
1179					parts = append(parts, line)
1180				}
1181				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1182			}
1183		}
1184		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1185		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1186			Sections:    built.Sections,
1187			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1188			OutputBytes: len(output),
1189			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1190			Harness:     actualHarness,
1191			Model:       actualModel,
1192		}
1193		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1194			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
1195			Rationale:        rationale,
1196			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1197			Findings:         findings,
1198			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1199			RawOutput:        output,
1200			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
1201			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
1202			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
1203			SessionID:        sessionID,
1204			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1205			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1206			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1207			DurationMS:       durationMS,
1208			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
1209			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
1210			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
1211		}
1212	
1213		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
1214		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
1215		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
1216		// regression. The result_rev is included in the body so the event can be
1217		// counted as an escalation trigger for subsequent dispatches.
1218		// Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the review outcome.
1219		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
1220			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1221				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
1222				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
1223				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower, resultRev),
1224				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1225				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1226			})
1227		}
1228		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1229			Harness:          actualHarness,
1230			Model:            actualModel,
1231			SessionID:        sessionID,
1232			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1233			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1234			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1235			BeadID:           beadID,
1236			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1237			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1238		}, reviewArtifactResult{
1239			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
1240			Rationale:        rationale,
1241			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1242			Findings:         findings,
1243			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1244			Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1245			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
1246		})
1247		if parseErr != nil {
1248			// FEAT-022 §12: distinguish provider_empty (zero bytes) from
1249			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators
1250			// triage these differently — provider_empty often signals a context-
1251			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
1252			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
1253			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1254			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
1255				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1256			}
1257			reviewRes.Error = class
1258			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
1259		}
1260		return reviewRes, nil
1261	}
1262	
1263	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
1264	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
1265	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
1266	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
1267	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
1268	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
1269	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
1270	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
1271		rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(r.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
1272		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, r.Service, r.Runner, rcfg, runtime)
1273	}
1274	
1275	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
1276	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
1277	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
1278	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
1279	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
1280		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1281		defer cancel()
1282		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1283		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1284		if err != nil {
1285			return "", err
1286		}
1287		return string(out), nil
1288	}
1289	
1290	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitDiff(baseRev, resultRev string) (string, error) {
1291		if strings.TrimSpace(baseRev) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(resultRev) == "" {
1292			return "", fmt.Errorf("base_rev and result_rev are required")
1293		}
1294		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1295		defer cancel()
1296		args := append([]string{"diff", baseRev, resultRev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1297		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1298		if err != nil {
1299			return "", err
1300		}
1301		return string(out), nil
1302	}
1303	
1304	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
1305		if resultRev == "" {
1306			return ""
1307		}
1308		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
1309		if err != nil {
1310			return ""
1311		}
1312		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1313	}
1314	
1315	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
1316		if artifacts == nil {
1317			return nil
1318		}
1319		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
1320			return err
1321		}
1322		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
1323	}
1324	
1325	func resultCost(result *Result) float64 {
1326		if result == nil {
1327			return 0
1328		}
1329		return result.CostUSD
1330	}
1331

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
8	)
9	
10	// ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind is the event kind emitted when a BLOCK verdict
11	// is classified as terminal (requires human intervention, no automated retry).
12	const ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind = "review-terminal-block"
13	
14	// Terminal review class constants. A BLOCK review verdict whose rationale
15	// contains one of these labels is mapped to a terminal operator-required
16	// outcome instead of re-entering the automated retry cycle.
17	const (
18		ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap           = "review_spec_gap"
19		ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance = "review_missing_acceptance"
20		ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge          = "review_too_large"
21		ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope  = "review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope"
22	)
23	
24	// classifyTerminalReviewBlock returns the terminal class label if structured
25	// review evidence identifies a human-required condition, or "" if the BLOCK is
26	// a regular fixable gap that may enter the automated retry cycle.
27	func classifyTerminalReviewBlock(res *ReviewResult) string {
28		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictBlock {
29			return ""
30		}
31		classification := ClassifyReviewFindings(res)
32		switch classification.Class {
33		case ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap:
34			return ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap
35		case ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance:
36			return ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance
37		case ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge:
38			return ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge
39		case ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope:
40			return ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope
41		default:
42			return ""
43		}
44	}
45	
46	// reviewTerminalBlockEventBody builds the structured body for a
47	// ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind event, encoding the terminal class and
48	// result_rev so operators can triage without re-parsing reviewer text.
49	func reviewTerminalBlockEventBody(class, resultRev string) string {
50		return fmt.Sprintf("terminal_class=%s\nresult_rev=%s", class, resultRev)
51	}
52	
53	// applyTerminalReviewBlock records terminal block state on a bead: moves it to
54	// status=proposed with operator-required metadata and appends a
55	// review-terminal-block event. All store errors are best-effort — callers
56	// continue regardless so a store-side failure cannot strand the bead.
57	func applyTerminalReviewBlock(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, class, resultRev string) {
58		applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(
59			store,
60			beadID,
61			actor,
62			now,
63			"terminal review block: "+class,
64			"review terminal block requires operator decision",
65			"review terminal BLOCK and accept, split, block, or cancel",
66		)
67		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
68			Kind:      ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind,
69			Summary:   class,
70			Body:      reviewTerminalBlockEventBody(class, resultRev),
71			Actor:     actor,
72			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
73			CreatedAt: now.UTC(),
74		})
75	}
76	
77	func applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, reason, summary, suggestedAction string) {
78		_ = store.ParkToProposed(beadID, bead.ParkReviewTerminal, func(b *bead.Bead) {
79			// Migration-only cleanup: defensive removal for legacy rows that escaped
80			// the lifecycle migration or arrived via external import.
81			b.Labels = removeBeadLabels(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, bead.LabelNeedsInvestigation)
82			clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b)
83			bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
84				Reason:          reason,
85				Since:           now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
86				Source:          "ddx agent execute-loop",
87				SuggestedAction: suggestedAction,
88				Summary:         summary,
89			})
90		})
91	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/triage"
16	)
17	
18	// triageDecisionEventKind is the event kind used to record the triage
19	// policy's chosen action after a non-APPROVE review verdict.
20	const triageDecisionEventKind = "triage-decision"
21	
22	// PostMergeReviewInput bundles the data RunPostMergeReview needs from its
23	// caller — historically the execute-bead loop body, now the per-attempt
24	// pipeline owned by try.Attempt (ddx-a921ff01 C3). The fields mirror what
25	// the inlined block read from the worker, the candidate bead, the report,
26	// and the loop runtime.
27	type PostMergeReviewInput struct {
28		Bead          bead.Bead
29		Report        ExecuteBeadReport
30		Reviewer      BeadReviewer
31		Store         ExecuteBeadLoopStore
32		ProjectRoot   string
33		Rcfg          config.ResolvedConfig
34		NoReview      bool
35		Log           io.Writer
36		Assignee      string
37		Now           func() time.Time
38		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
39	}
40	
41	// PostMergeReviewOutput carries the review state machine's decision back to
42	// the caller. Report is the (possibly updated) ExecuteBeadReport; Approved
43	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
44	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected
45	// to route StoreErr through its outcome-error handler
46	// (handleOutcomeStoreError on ExecuteBeadWorker), keyed by StoreErrOp.
47	//
48	// RepairContext is non-nil when the BLOCK verdict was classified as a fixable
49	// gap and exactly one repair cycle has been scheduled for this result_rev.
50	// The caller should run a repair attempt with MinPower above
51	// RepairContext.ImplementerActualPower while preserving all explicit
52	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
53	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
54		Report        ExecuteBeadReport

61	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
62	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
63	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
64	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
65	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
66	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
67	// review events without reopening the bead.

70	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
71	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
72	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
73	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
74	// still consulted without reopening the bead.
75	//

77	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
78	// reviewer transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp +
79	// StoreErr so callers can keep the existing handleOutcomeStoreError pattern

2999	// isValidImplementationAttempt reports whether the given report came from a
3000	// real implementation attempt — one where the implementer had checkout context
3001	// established and a result that can be compared against the base revision for
3002	// no-progress accounting. Returns false for system failures, infrastructure
3003	// issues, intake/scheduling outcomes, review/operator-managed states, and
3004	// other non-implementation outcomes that should not consume the no-progress
3005	// retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.
3006	func isValidImplementationAttempt(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
3007		if report.Disrupted {
3008			return false
3009		}
3010		if isTransientOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
3011			return false
3012		}
3013		// Extended system-failure and non-implementation outcome reasons.
3014		switch report.OutcomeReason {
3015		case "preflight_failed",
3016			"intake_block",
3017			"claim_race",
3018			"actionable_but_rewritten",
3019			"too_large_decomposed",
3020			"intake_error",
3021			"ambiguous_needs_human",
3022			FailureModeAuthError,
3023			FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled,
3024			FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied,
3025			FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint,
3026			FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced,
3027			FailureModeWorktreeLost,
3028			ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
3029			ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency,
3030			"operator_required":
3031			return false
3032		}
3033		// Operator/review status codes where the implementer did not run a full
3034		// implementation pass.
3035		switch report.Status {
3036		case ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction,
3037			ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition,
3038			ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired,
3039			ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock:
3040			return false
3041		}
3042		// Without a BaseRev the implementer never had a commit baseline.
3043		if report.BaseRev == "" {
3044			return false
3045		}
3046		return true
3047	}
3048	
3049	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
3050		if !isValidImplementationAttempt(report) {
3051			return false
3052		}
3053		if report.ResultRev == "" {
