3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	)
9	
10	// ExecuteBeadIterationBlock is the machine-readable JSON payload embedded in
11	// execute-bead iteration commit messages as the "ddx-iteration:" trailer value.
12	// All fields are projected from result.json; the struct is the canonical source
13	// for both commit-message rendering and forward-compatibility testing.
14	type ExecuteBeadIterationBlock struct {
15		BeadID              string  `json:"bead_id"`
16		AttemptID           string  `json:"attempt_id"`
17		SessionID           string  `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
18		Harness             string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
19		Provider            string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
20		Model               string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
21		TotalTokens         int     `json:"total_tokens"`
22		CostUSD             float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
23		BaseRev             string  `json:"base_rev"`
24		ResultRev           string  `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
25		RequiredExecSummary string  `json:"required_exec_summary"`
26		Outcome             string  `json:"outcome"`
27		ExecutionBundle     string  `json:"execution_bundle"`
28	}
29	
30	// BuildIterationCommitMessage renders the canonical execute-bead iteration
31	// commit message from res. All values are projected from the result — the same
32	// fields that are written to result.json — so the message is re-derivable from
33	// the tracked artifact file without re-running the agent.
34	//
35	// The message includes:
36	//   - A structured subject line referencing the bead ID
37	//   - The ddx-iteration JSON block (compact agent/provider metadata)
38	//   - The canonical Git trailer set (Ddx-Attempt-Id, Ddx-Worker-Id,
39	//     Ddx-Harness, Ddx-Model, Ddx-Result-Status)
40	//
41	// Unknown fields are handled deterministically: ResultRev is omitted when
42	// empty; RequiredExecSummary defaults to "skipped" when unset; WorkerID falls
43	// back to SessionID when no distinct worker identity exists.
44	func BuildIterationCommitMessage(res *ExecuteBeadResult) string {
45		block := ExecuteBeadIterationBlock{
46			BeadID:              res.BeadID,
47			AttemptID:           res.AttemptID,
48			SessionID:           res.SessionID,
49			Harness:             res.Harness,
50			Provider:            res.Provider,
51			Model:               res.Model,
52			TotalTokens:         res.Tokens,
53			CostUSD:             res.CostUSD,
54			BaseRev:             res.BaseRev,
55			ResultRev:           res.ResultRev,
56			RequiredExecSummary: iterationRequiredExecSummary(res),
57			Outcome:             res.Outcome,
58			ExecutionBundle:     res.ExecutionDir,
59		}
60	
61		blockJSON, err := json.MarshalIndent(block, "", "  ")
62		if err != nil {
63			// Fallback: minimal inline block so the commit is never trailer-free.
64			blockJSON = []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"bead_id":%q,"attempt_id":%q}`, res.BeadID, res.AttemptID))
65		}
66	
67		// Ddx-Worker-Id falls back to SessionID when no distinct worker identity exists.
68		workerID := res.WorkerID
69		if workerID == "" {
70			workerID = res.SessionID
71		}
72	
73		var b strings.Builder
74		b.WriteString("chore: execute-bead iteration ")
75		b.WriteString(res.BeadID)
76		b.WriteString("\n\nddx-iteration: ")
77		b.WriteString(string(blockJSON))
78		// Blank line between the JSON block and the canonical trailer set is required
79		// by git-interpret-trailers so that the trailers are parsed correctly.
80		b.WriteString("\n\nDdx-Attempt-Id: ")
81		b.WriteString(res.AttemptID)
82		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Worker-Id: ")
83		b.WriteString(workerID)
84		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Harness: ")
85		b.WriteString(res.Harness)
86		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Model: ")
87		b.WriteString(res.Model)
88		b.WriteString("\nDdx-Result-Status: ")
89		b.WriteString(res.Status)
90		return b.String()
91	}
92	
93	// iterationRequiredExecSummary returns the required_exec_summary value for the
94	// ddx-iteration block. When the orchestrator has not yet evaluated gates (i.e.
95	// the preliminary result is written before the synthesized commit), the field
96	// defaults to "skipped" per the documented contract. After ApplyLandingToResult
97	// the field is populated with the real summary and the final result.json is
98	// written.
99	func iterationRequiredExecSummary(res *ExecuteBeadResult) string {
100		if res.RequiredExecSummary != "" {
101			return res.RequiredExecSummary
102		}
103		return "skipped"
104	}
105	
106	// BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile reads the result.json artifact at resultPath
107	// and renders the canonical execute-bead iteration commit message from it.
108	// This is the file-sourced rendering path: callers must write result.json before
109	// calling this function. The message is deterministic for a given result.json —
110	// anyone can re-derive it from the tracked bundle without re-running the agent.
111	func BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(resultPath string) (string, error) {
112		raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
113		if err != nil {
114			return "", fmt.Errorf("reading result file %s: %w", resultPath, err)
115		}
116		var res ExecuteBeadResult
117		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
118			return "", fmt.Errorf("parsing result file %s: %w", resultPath, err)
119		}
120		return BuildIterationCommitMessage(&res), nil
121	}

170	// ExecuteBeadRuntime carries the non-durable plumbing for an execute-bead
171	// run: per-invocation intent (FromRev, PromptFile, WorkerID) and
172	// non-serializable injection seams (BeadEvents, Service, AgentRunner).
173	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
174	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed
175	// via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's rcfg argument.

  Sister bead (ddx-c739d7ec) introduces `ddx bead ac-check` —
  a mechanical per-AC verification tool that produces
  `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json`. This bead
  changes the reviewer behavior to:
    1. Invoke ac-check during pre-commit so implementer can
       self-check before submitting (closes information
       asymmetry).
    2. Pass ac-check.json into the reviewer prompt as
       structured input. Reviewer ratifies or overrides;
       does not re-grep the diff independently.
    3. Add REQUEST_CLARIFICATION verdict for prose AC items
       — does NOT BLOCK the queue; pings operator.
    4. Strictness modes driven by bead labels: kind:fix /
       kind:feat = strict; kind:refactor / kind:chore =
       behavior-light; kind:doc / kind:mechanical = file
       evidence only.

  Without this bead, ac-check is descriptive only —
  implementer can ignore it, reviewer doesn't consume it.

CURRENT STATE
  - Sister bead ddx-c739d7ec lands the ac-check tool but does
    not wire it into the implementer or reviewer flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-234 emits
    per_ac[].grade + per_ac[].evidence with no structured
    input — reviewer greps raw diff.
  - Reviewer verdicts: APPROVE, BLOCK. No REQUEST_CLARIFICATION.
  - Reviewer strictness is uniform — no per-kind tuning.

PROPOSED FIX
  Three coordinated changes:

  1. Wire ac-check into execute-bead implementer flow:
     - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (or execute_bead_loop.go):
       after staging changes and BEFORE creating the
       implementer's commit, invoke `ddx bead ac-check <id>`.
     - Capture the ac-check.json into the attempt's evidence
       dir.
     - The implementer commit's body must include an
       `AC-Check:` trailer summarizing the result (e.g.,
       `AC-Check: 3 pass / 1 fail / 1 needs-judgment`).
     - If `fail` count > 0 without an explicit `AC-Waive: #N
       <reason>` trailer in the commit body, abort the attempt
       and route through the existing escalation path.

  2. Update reviewer prompt to consume ac-check.json:
     - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go
       beadReviewInstructions (or the canonical prompt
       template): inject ac-check.json as a `<ac-check>`
       section.
     - Reviewer's job becomes: ratify the mechanical results
       per AC, OR override with explicit
       `judgment_override.reason`. Override requires concrete
       diff citation.
     - Reviewer adjudicates `needs-judgment` items
       (judgment-only AC) and chooses pass/fail/clarify.
     - New verdict: REQUEST_CLARIFICATION. Used when the
       reviewer cannot adjudicate a needs-judgment item
       without more bead context. Routes to the operator
       lane (status=proposed with explanatory label) — does
       NOT block the drain queue further.

  3. Strictness modes by bead label:
     - kind:fix, kind:feat → strict (default; current behavior).
     - kind:refactor, kind:chore → behavior-light: pass = build
       green + symbol/file evidence; do not require test-name
       match unless AC explicitly names a Test*.
     - kind:doc, kind:mechanical → mechanical: file/rename/
       symbol evidence only.
     - Reviewer reads b.Labels and applies the mode by
       picking different prompt-section templates.

     - TestReviewer_RatifiesACCheck_AllPass_ApproveImplicit
     - TestReviewer_RatifiesACCheck_Fail_BlocksByRatification
     - TestReviewer_NeedsJudgment_AdjudicatedOrClarification
     - TestReviewer_RequestClarification_NotBlockingQueue
     - TestReviewer_OverridePassToBlock_RequiresJudgmentReason
     - TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindFix_RequiresTestNames
     - TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindRefactor_BuildGreenSuffices
     - TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindDoc_FilePresenceSuffices
     - TestImplementerFlow_ACCheckFail_AbortsWithoutWaive
     - TestImplementerFlow_ACWaiveTrailer_PassesAttempt
     - TestReviewer_RegressionFakeRetainDays_BlocksByACCheck

NON-SCOPE
  - The ac-check tool itself (sister bead ddx-c739d7ec).
  - Implementer prompt changes (out of scope; the AC-Check
    trailer requirement is added at the orchestrator level).
  - Manual operator override tools.

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: sister bead ddx-c739d7ec (ac-check tool) must
    land first.

    asymmetry principle aligns with the strictness-by-kind

1	// Package accheck mechanically verifies acceptance criteria against the
2	// working tree and diff. It is invoked by `ddx bead ac-check <id>` to produce
3	// a structured per-AC result file that both implementer (self-check
4	// pre-commit) and reviewer (gate ratification) consume.
5	//
6	// Per ddx-c739d7ec: the tool classifies each AC line into a kind
7	// (test-name | build-gate | negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) then
8	// resolves evidence mechanically. Prose AC always yields needs_judgment;
9	// never auto-pass, never auto-fail.
10	package accheck
11	
12	import (
13		"bufio"
14		"fmt"
15		"io"
16		"os/exec"
17		"path/filepath"
18		"regexp"
19		"strings"
20	)
21	
22	// SchemaVersion is bumped when the output JSON shape changes incompatibly.
23	const SchemaVersion = 1
24	
25	// Kind classifies an AC line by the evidence we know how to gather for it.
26	type Kind string
27	
28	const (
29		KindTestName   Kind = "test-name"
30		KindBuildGate  Kind = "build-gate"
31		KindNegative   Kind = "negative"
32		KindSymbol     Kind = "symbol"
33		KindMechanical Kind = "mechanical"
34		KindProse      Kind = "prose"
35	)
36	
37	// Result is the verdict on one AC item.
38	type Result string
39	
40	const (
41		ResultPass          Result = "pass"
42		ResultFail          Result = "fail"
43		ResultNeedsJudgment Result = "needs_judgment"
44		ResultError         Result = "error"
45	)
46	
47	// Item represents one parsed AC line with its classification.
48	type Item struct {
49		AC      int    // 1-based AC number
50		Text    string // original AC text (single line)
51		Kind    Kind   // classified kind
52		Name    string // extracted entity (test name, symbol, etc.); empty for prose
53		Alias   string // operator-declared substitution (e.g., TestFoo -> TestFoo/bar)
54	}
55	
56	// Output is the structured per-AC verification output written to
57	// .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json.
58	type Output struct {
59		SchemaVersion int     `json:"schema_version"`
60		BeadID        string  `json:"bead_id"`
61		AttemptID     string  `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
62		Summary       Summary `json:"summary"`
63		Items         []Entry `json:"items"`
64	}
65	
66	// Summary aggregates counts across items.
67	type Summary struct {
68		Pass          int `json:"pass"`
69		Fail          int `json:"fail"`
70		NeedsJudgment int `json:"needs_judgment"`
71		Error         int `json:"error"`
72	}
73	
74	// Entry is one per-AC result in the structured output.
75	type Entry struct {
76		AC       int    `json:"ac"`
77		Kind     Kind   `json:"kind"`
78		Name     string `json:"name,omitempty"`
79		Result   Result `json:"result"`
80		Evidence string `json:"evidence"`

26	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
27	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
28	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.
32	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
33		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
34		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
35		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
36		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
37		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
38		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
39		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
40		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
41		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
42		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
43		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
46		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
48		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
49	
50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
10	
11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
24	
25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
29	const (
30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
40		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
41		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
42		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
43		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
44		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
45		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
46	)
47	
48	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
49	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
50	// queue.
51	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
52	
53	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
54	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
55	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
56	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
57	//
58	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
59	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
60	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
61	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
62	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
63	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
64		switch outcome {
65		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
66			if exitCode == 0 {
67				return ""
68			}
69			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
70			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
71		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
72			return FailureModeNoChanges
73		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
74			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
75		}
76	
77		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
78		switch {
79		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
80			return FailureModeLockContention
81		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
82			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
83		case containsAny(lower,
84			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
85			"passthrough constraint:",
86			"max_power is less than min_power",
87			"max_power exceeds min_power",
88			"must be less than max_power",
89			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
90			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
91			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
92			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
93			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
94			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
95		case containsAny(lower,
96			"agent power unsatisfied",
97			"no model meets min_power",
98			"no model with power >=",
99			"minimum power not achievable",
100			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
101			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
102		case containsAny(lower,
103			"executable file not found in $path",
104			"executable file not found",
105			"no such file or directory") &&
106			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
107			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
108		case containsAny(lower,
109			"no viable harness",
110			"no harness configured",
111			"failed to initialize routing service",
112			"resolveroute:",
113			"no viable routing candidate",
114			"no live provider supports",
115			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
116			"no viable provider"):
117			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
118		case containsAny(lower,
119			"context length",
120			"context_length_exceeded",
121			"context window",
122			"maximum context",
123			"prompt is too long",
124			"prompt too long",
125			"token limit",
126			"too many tokens",
127			"exceeds the context"):
128			return FailureModeContextOverflow
129		case containsAny(lower,
130			"unauthorized",
131			"401 unauthorized",
132			"invalid api key",
133			"invalid_api_key",
134			"authentication failed",
135			"authentication_error",
136			"no api key",
137			"missing api key",
138			"quota exceeded",
139			"insufficient quota",
140			"insufficient_quota",
141			"rate limit",
142			"rate_limit",
143			"429"):
144			return FailureModeAuthError
145		case containsAny(lower,
146			"timeout",
147			"timed out",
148			"context deadline exceeded",
149			"deadline exceeded",
150			"signal: killed"):
151			return FailureModeTimeout
152		case containsAny(lower,
153			"merge conflict",
154			"ff-merge not possible",
155			"merge failed",
156			"automatic merge failed"):
157			return FailureModeMergeConflict
158		case containsAny(lower,
159			"build failed",
160			"compilation failed",
161			"compile error",
162			"compilation error",
163			"cannot find package",
164			"undefined:",
165			"undefined reference",
166			"syntax error"):
167			return FailureModeBuildFailure
168		case containsAny(lower,
169			"test failed",
170			"tests failed",
171			"--- fail:",
172			"assertion failed",
173			"test failure"):
174			return FailureModeTestFailure
175		}
176		if outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed && errMsg != "" {
177			return FailureModeUnknown
178		}
179		if exitCode != 0 {
180			return FailureModeUnknown
181		}
182		return ""
183	}
184	
185	// IsWorktreeLostError reports whether errMsg describes the execute-bead
186	// infrastructure losing the isolated attempt worktree before DDx could inspect
187	// its HEAD. This deliberately requires both a HEAD/worktree signal and a
188	// missing-path signal so generic harness "no such file" errors do not get
189	// bucketed as infrastructure loss.
190	func IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg string) bool {
191		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
192		hasHeadSignal := containsAny(lower,
193			"failed to read worktree head",
194			"git rev-parse head",
195			"worktree path missing")
196		hasMissingPathSignal := containsAny(lower,
197			"chdir",
198			"no such file or directory",
199			"cannot access",
200			"does not exist")
201		return hasHeadSignal && hasMissingPathSignal
202	}
203	
204	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
205	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
206	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
207		for _, n := range needles {
208			if n == "" {
209				continue
210			}
211			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
212				return true
213			}
214		}
215		return false
216	}
217	
218	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
219	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
220	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
221	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
222	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
223	// bead merged cleanly.
224	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
225		switch landingOutcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ""
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return FailureModeNoChanges
230		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
231			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
232		case "error":
233			if workerMode != "" {
234				return workerMode
235			}
236			return FailureModeUnknown
237		case "preserved":
238			switch {
239			case landingReason == "merge conflict" || landingReason == "merge failed" || landingReason == "ff-merge not possible":
240				return FailureModeMergeConflict
241			case landingReason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(landingReason, PreMergeChecksReason):
242				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
243			case landingReason == RatchetPreserveReason:
244				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
245			case landingReason == OperatorCancelReason:
246				return ""
247			}
248			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
249			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
250			if workerMode != "" {
251				return workerMode
252			}
253			return FailureModeUnknown
254		}
255		if workerMode != "" {
256			return workerMode
257		}
258		return FailureModeUnknown
259	}
260	
261	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
262	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
263	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
264	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
265	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
266	// test-oriented by convention.
267	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
268		for _, gr := range gateResults {
269			if gr.Status != "fail" {
270				continue
271			}
272			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
273			if containsAny(combined,
274				"build failed",
275				"compilation failed",
276				"compile error",
277				"compilation error",
278				"cannot find package",
279				"undefined:",
280				"undefined reference",
281				"syntax error") {
282				return FailureModeBuildFailure
283			}
284		}
285		return FailureModeTestFailure
286	}

288	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
289	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
290	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
291	const (
292		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
293		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
294		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
295		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
296	)
297	
298	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
299	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
300	const (
301		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
302		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
303		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
304		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
305		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
306		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
307		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
308		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
309		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
310		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
311		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
312		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
313		ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted          = "resource_exhausted"
314	
315		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
316		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
317		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
318		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
319		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
320		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
321		//
322		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
323		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
324		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
325		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
326		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
327		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
328	
329		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
330		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
331		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
332		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
333		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
334	
335		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
336		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
337		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
338		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
339		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
340	
341		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired is set when the focused
342		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
343		// operator judgment, not automated retry. The bead moves to status=proposed
344		// with a kind:land-conflict-operator-required event.
345		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired = "land_conflict_operator_required"
346	
347		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock is set when a BLOCK review verdict
348		// is classified as a terminal operator-required condition —
349		// review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth
350		// cap, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope. These classes do not re-enter
351		// the automated retry cycle and do not consume the no-progress retry
352		// budget; the bead moves to status=proposed until an operator
353		// intervenes.
354		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock = "review_terminal_block"
355	
356		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap is set when a BLOCK review verdict is
357		// classified as a fixable gap — the issues are addressable by a repair
358		// attempt at higher MinPower with the reviewer's rationale as context.
359		// Exactly one repair cycle is scheduled per result_rev; a second
360		// review_fixable_gap on the same result_rev falls through to the regular
361		// BLOCK triage path instead.
362		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap = "review_fixable_gap"
363	
364		// ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted is set when the candidate-cycle
365		// repair loop reaches its per-attempt repair_max_cycles limit before a
366		// reviewer approves the candidate. The latest candidate ref is retained for
367		// operator inspection.
368		ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted = "repair-cycle-exhausted"
369	
370		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestClarification is set when the reviewer
371		// returns REQUEST_CLARIFICATION for a needs-judgment item it cannot
372		// adjudicate without more bead context. The bead is parked to proposed
373		// (operator lane) without blocking the drain queue.
374		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestClarification = "review_request_clarification"
375	)
376	
377	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
378	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
379	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
380	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
381		if exitCode != 0 {
382			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
383		}
384	
385		switch outcome {
386		case "merged":
387			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
388			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
389			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
390			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
391			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
392			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
393				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
394			}
395			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
396				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
397			}
398			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
399		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
400			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
401		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
402			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
403		case "error":
404			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
405		case "preserved":
406			switch {
407			case reason == "merge conflict" || reason == "merge failed" || reason == "ff-merge not possible":
408				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
409			case reason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(reason, PreMergeChecksReason):
410				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
411			case reason == RatchetPreserveReason:
412				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
413			case reason == OperatorCancelReason:
414				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
415			}
416			if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
417				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
418			}
419			// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
420			// explicitly requested no merge.
421			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
422		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
423			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
424		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
425			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
426		default:
427			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
428		}
429	}
430	
431	// IsResourceExhaustedStatus reports whether the status is the stable
432	// host-resource failure classification.
433	func IsResourceExhaustedStatus(status string) bool {
434		return status == ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted
435	}
436	
437	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
438		for _, prefix := range []string{
439			"evidence commit failed:",
440			"large-deletion gate:",
441			"syntax sanity gate:",
442			"post-land gate failed:",
443		} {
444			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
445				return true
446			}
447		}
448		return false
449	}
450	
451	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
452		switch {
453		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
454			return reason + ": " + errMsg
455		case reason != "":
456			return reason
457		case errMsg != "":
458			return errMsg
459		case status != "":
460			return status
461		default:
462			return "unknown"
463		}
464	}

130	func commitReviewFixtureFile(t *testing.T, projectRoot, name, body string) string {
131		t.Helper()
132		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, name), []byte(body), 0o644))
133		out, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "add", name).CombinedOutput()
134		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
135		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@example.com", "commit", "-m", "candidate "+name).CombinedOutput()
136		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
137		headRaw, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
138		require.NoError(t, err)
139		return strings.TrimSpace(string(headRaw))
140	}

162	// makeReviewer builds a beadReviewerFunc that always returns the given verdict.
163	func makeReviewer(verdict Verdict, output string) beadReviewerFunc {
164		return beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
165			res := &ReviewResult{
166				Verdict:         verdict,
167				RawOutput:       output,
168				ReviewerHarness: "claude",
169				ReviewerModel:   "claude-opus-4-6",
170			}
171			if verdict == VerdictApprove {
172				res.PerAC = []ReviewAC{
173					{

249	func TestRunPostMergeReviewChargesReviewCostAndDefersWhenCapTrips(t *testing.T) {
250		cases := []struct {
251			name         string
252			maxCost      float64
253			preCost      float64
254			reviewCost   float64
255			wantDeferred bool
256			wantApproved bool
257		}{
258			{
259				name:         "normal fit",
260				maxCost:      10.0,
261				preCost:      4.0,
262				reviewCost:   1.5,
263				wantDeferred: false,
264				wantApproved: true,
265			},
266			{
267				name:         "review tips over budget",
268				maxCost:      5.0,
269				preCost:      4.0,
270				reviewCost:   1.5,
271				wantDeferred: true,
272				wantApproved: false,
273			},
274		}
275		for _, tc := range cases {
276			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
277				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
278				tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(tc.maxCost, func(string) bool { return true })
279				tracker.Add("openrouter", tc.preCost)
280	
281				reviewer := makeReviewerWithCost(VerdictApprove, "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nAll good.", tc.reviewCost)
282				out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
283					Bead: *first,
284					Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
285						BeadID:    first.ID,
286						Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
287						SessionID: "sess-review-cost",
288						ResultRev: "cafebabe",
289						CostUSD:   tc.preCost,
290					},
291					Reviewer:      reviewer,
292					Store:         store,
293					ProjectRoot:   t.TempDir(),
294					Rcfg:          config.NewTestConfigForLoop(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"})),
295					Now:           time.Now,
296					Assignee:      "worker",
297					ReviewCostCap: tracker,
298				})
299				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantApproved, out.Approved)
300				require.NoError(t, out.StoreErr)
301				assert.InDelta(t, tc.preCost+tc.reviewCost, tracker.Spent(), 1e-9)
302	
303				events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
304				require.NoError(t, err)
305				foundDeferred := false
306				for _, ev := range events {
307					if ev.Kind == "review-cost-deferred" {
308						foundDeferred = true
309						assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "result_rev=cafebabe")
310					}
311				}
312				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantDeferred, foundDeferred)
313			})
314		}
315	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"testing"
7	
8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
9	)

11	// TestSynthesizeCommit_GitignoredDirsDoNotFail covers ddx-feb1d4a5:
12	// RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit must not fail with "staging changes: exit
13	// status 1" when .ddx/agent-logs/, .ddx/workers/, or .ddx/executions/
14	// exist as untracked gitignored directories. Previously the :(exclude)
15	// pathspecs for these paths caused `git add` to report them as
16	// explicitly-ignored and exit non-zero.
17	func TestSynthesizeCommit_GitignoredDirsDoNotFail(t *testing.T) {
18		root, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 0)
19	
20		gitignore := filepath.Join(root, ".gitignore")
21		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(gitignore,
22			[]byte(".ddx/agent-logs/\n.ddx/workers/\n.ddx/executions/\n"), 0644))
23		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".gitignore")
24		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add gitignore")
25	
26		logsDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
27		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logsDir, 0755))
28		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(logsDir, "log.jsonl"),
29			[]byte(`{"ts":1}`), 0644))
30	
31		workersDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "workers")
32		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0755))
33		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workersDir, "w.json"),
34			[]byte(`{}`), 0644))
35	
36		executionsDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "attempt", "embedded")
37		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(executionsDir, 0755))
38		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(executionsDir, "session.jsonl"),
39			[]byte(`{"ts":1}`), 0644))
40	
41		ops := &RealGitOps{}
42	
43		committed, err := ops.SynthesizeCommit(root, "chore: test checkpoint")
44		require.NoError(t, err, "SynthesizeCommit must succeed when only untracked changes are in gitignored dirs")
45		require.False(t, committed, "no commit expected when the only 'changes' are gitignored")
46	
47		realFile := filepath.Join(root, "feature.txt")
48		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(realFile, []byte("feature\n"), 0644))
49	
50		committed, err = ops.SynthesizeCommit(root, "chore: test real change")
51		require.NoError(t, err, "SynthesizeCommit must succeed with a real change alongside gitignored dirs")
52		require.True(t, committed, "commit expected when a real tracked-or-untracked file changes")
53	
54		trackedOut := runGitInteg(t, root, "ls-tree", "-r", "--name-only", "HEAD")
55		require.Contains(t, trackedOut, "feature.txt", "real change must land in the commit")
56		require.NotContains(t, trackedOut, ".ddx/agent-logs", "gitignored path must not be committed")
57		require.NotContains(t, trackedOut, ".ddx/workers", "gitignored path must not be committed")
58		require.NotContains(t, trackedOut, ".ddx/executions", "gitignored path must not be committed")
59	}

15	// TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress verifies that each
16	// terminal review class (spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large,
17	// unsafe_or_out_of_scope) is correctly classified as a terminal operator-
18	// required outcome. Key assertions:
19	//   - Report status is ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock.
20	//   - Bead moves to status=proposed with operator-required metadata.
21	//   - A review-terminal-block event is appended with the correct class.
22	//   - isValidImplementationAttempt returns false → no no-progress budget consumed.
23	//   - Bead is NOT closed.
24	func TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress(t *testing.T) {
25		classes := []struct {
26			name  string
27			class string
28		}{
29			{"spec_gap", ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap},
30			{"missing_acceptance", ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance},
31			{"too_large", ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge},
32			{"unsafe_or_out_of_scope", ReviewTerminalClassUnsafeOrOutScope},
33		}
34		for _, tc := range classes {
35			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
36				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
37				reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
38					return &ReviewResult{
39						Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
40						Rationale:       "This BLOCK is terminal: " + tc.class,
41						RawOutput:       "BLOCK: " + tc.class,
42						ReviewerHarness: "claude",
43						ReviewerModel:   "claude-opus-4-6",
44						Findings: []Finding{
45							{Severity: "block", Summary: "Structured terminal class: " + tc.class, Location: "bead:AC1"},
46						},
47					}, nil
48				})
49	
50				cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
51				rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
52				out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
53					Bead: *first,
54					Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
55						BeadID:    first.ID,
56						Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
57						SessionID: "sess-terminal-" + tc.name,
58						ResultRev: "aabbcc01",
59						BaseRev:   "aabbcc00",
60					},
61					Reviewer:    reviewer,
62					Store:       store,
63					ProjectRoot: t.TempDir(),
64					Rcfg:        rcfg,
65					Now:         time.Now,
66					Assignee:    "worker",
67				})
68				require.False(t, out.Approved)
69	
70				// Must count as a failure, not a success.
71				assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock, out.Report.Status)
72	
73				// Bead must move to operator attention (not closed).
74				got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
75				require.NoError(t, err)
76				assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusProposed, got.Status, "terminal block must move to proposed")
77				assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "terminal block must not close the bead")
78	
79				// Bead must use status-owned operator metadata, not legacy labels.
80				assert.NotContains(t, got.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel,
81					"terminal block must not add needs_human label (class=%s)", tc.class)
82				meta := bead.GetNeedsHumanMeta(*got)
83				assert.Contains(t, meta.Reason, tc.class)
84				assert.Equal(t, "ddx agent execute-loop", meta.Source)
85	
86				// review-terminal-block event must be appended with the correct class.
87				events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
88				require.NoError(t, err)
89				foundTerminal := false
90				for _, ev := range events {
91					if ev.Kind == ReviewTerminalBlockEventKind && ev.Summary == tc.class {
92						foundTerminal = true
93						assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "terminal_class="+tc.class)
94						assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "result_rev=aabbcc01")
95					}
96				}
97				assert.True(t, foundTerminal,
98					"expected review-terminal-block event with class %s", tc.class)
99	
100				// isValidImplementationAttempt must return false → no no-progress budget.
101				assert.False(t, isValidImplementationAttempt(ExecuteBeadReport{
102					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock,
103					BaseRev:   "aabbcc00",
104					ResultRev: "aabbcc01",
105				}), "review_terminal_block must not consume the no-progress retry budget")
106			})
107		}
108	}
109	
110	// TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorProposed verifies that
111	// when review-error events exhaust the retry budget, the bead moves to
112	// status=proposed alongside the terminal review-manual-required event. This
113	// ensures exhausted review errors park the bead with operator-required metadata
114	// rather than silently cycling.
115	func TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorProposed(t *testing.T) {
116		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
117		const resultRev = "cafebabe"
118	
119		// Seed N-1 prior failures (default N=3 → 2 prior events). The next
120		// reviewer attempt becomes the 3rd, which must trip the terminal event.
121		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, resultRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty, 2)
122	
123		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
124		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
125		out := runPostMergeReviewRetryFixture(t, store, first, resultRev, failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty), rcfg)
126		require.False(t, out.Approved)
127	
128		// review-manual-required event must be present with the correct class.
129		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131		var manual *bead.BeadEvent
132		for i := range events {
133			if events[i].Kind == "review-manual-required" {
134				manual = &events[i]
135			}
136		}
137		require.NotNil(t, manual, "exhausted review_error must emit a review-manual-required event")
138		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "failure_class="+evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
139			"review-manual-required body must carry failure_class")
140		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "attempt_count=3",
141			"review-manual-required body must carry attempt_count")
142		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "result_rev="+resultRev,
143			"review-manual-required body must carry result_rev")
144	
145		// Operator parking must be status-owned on exhausted review error.
146		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
147		require.NoError(t, err)
148		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusProposed, got.Status,
149			"exhausted review_error must move bead to proposed for operator triage")
150		assert.NotContains(t, got.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel,
151			"exhausted review_error must not use needs_human label parking")
152		meta := bead.GetNeedsHumanMeta(*got)
153		assert.Contains(t, meta.Reason, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty)
154		assert.Equal(t, "ddx agent execute-loop", meta.Source)
155	
156		// Bead must NOT be closed by reviewer-failure escalation.
157		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
158			"reviewer failure (even at terminal escalation) must never close the bead")
159	}

data.text:
The commit landed cleanly. The implementation is complete:

- **`bead/park.go`**: Added `ParkReviewRequestClarification` reason + metadata map entry
- **`execute_bead_status.go`**: Added `ExecuteBeadStatusACCheckFailed` status constant
- **`review_verdict.go`**: Added `VerdictRequestClarification` and updated `ParseReviewVerdict` to accept it
- **`execute_bead_review.go`**: New `beadReviewInstructions` with strictness mode/AC-check ratification/REQUEST_CLARIFICATION; `beadStrictnessMode()` helper; `ACCheckJSON` field in `BuildReviewPromptOptions`; `<ac-check>` section injection; `readACCheckJSON()` helper; `reviewBeadWithDiff` reads ac-check.json via `impl.Correlation["attempt_id"]`
- **`execute_bead_review_terminal.go`**: `ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind` + `applyReviewRequestClarification()` (parks via `ParkReviewRequestClarification`, appends event)
- **`execute_bead_post_review.go`**: `VerdictRequestClarification` case in the verdict switch
- **`execute_bead_loop.go`**: `isValidImplementationAttempt` excludes the two new status codes
- **`execute_bead.go`**: `ACCheckRunner` field on `ExecuteBeadRuntime`; pre-commit gate writes `ac-check.json`, appends `AC-Check:` trailer, aborts on unwaived fails; `allFailsWaived`/`readWaiveSet`/`buildACWaiveTrailers` helpers
- **`execute_bead_review_test.go`**: All 11 named tests pass
- **`store_test.go`**: Updated `TestParkToProposed` to include the new park reason
- **`collapse_golden.txt`**: Regenerated to match new review instructions
